title: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@cardiohealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through

Liam / CardioHealth team — attaching an activation dependency matrix for the POC onramp. This is a lightweight MAP-focused doc that lays out the minimum sequencing we need to hit a production-like test in your staging (API keys, org + tenant setup, IP allowlist, and VPC peering).

Top-level asks: 
- Confirm the IP CIDR ranges we should add to the allowlist (we've proposed 52.22.44.0/24 and 18.197.128.0/25 as a short-lived window for the first two sprints).
- Will CardioHealth initiate the VPC peering request or should Redwood create a peering and share the ID (we can support both; if you prefer to initiate, share the contact).
- Org setup: we need a tenant name and an owner email (read/write for first 30d).
- Ephemeral API key pattern: propose 72-hour TTL during eval; confirm acceptable.

Attached: activation-dependency-matrix.xlsx (sheet 1 = milestones, sheet 2 = owners/contact points).

Proposed milestone cadence (high level):
1) Org + owner assigned (day 0)
2) Short-lived API key + smoke test (day 1)
3) IP allowlist applied + test (day 1–2)
4) VPC peering (optional, day 3–6)
5) Canary load + evaluation queries (day 7–14)

If you want, Jonas can walk through the matrix with your netops team tomorrow — propose a 30-min slot.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: activation-dependency-matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet stub, 56KB)


From: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@cardiohealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: netops@cardiohealth.com
Date: Thu, 14 Oct 2026 11:01:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through

Ava — thanks for the matrix and clear asks. A few quick notes from NetOps: 
- CIDR ranges: we can open the 52.22.44.0/24 and 18.197.128.0/25 windows for an initial 72-hour trial, but will need the exact source IPs once sessions stabilize.
- VPC peering: CardioHealth typically accepts incoming peering requests (we prefer to initiate to keep our routing strict). I'll have netops create the request if you provide the Redwood VPC ID and proposed routing rules.
- Org owner: use onboarding@cardiohealth.com as the temporary owner and we will switch to the permanent owner when legal finalizes the tenant agreement.
- Ephemeral keys: 72h TTL is fine for the trial. We will need instructions on rotating keys if you expect rolling restarts.

MM: netops@cardiohealth.com — I'll leave you to coordinate peering logistics with Jonas.

I'll confirm the peering window after sync with netops; tentatively aiming for Mon Oct 18 to Thu Oct 21.

— Liam
Head of Integrations, CardioHealth Labs


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@cardiohealth.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Oct 2026 08:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through

Looping in Jonas for the VPC peering coordination. Our commercial timeline is unchanged: we want the trial active by the week of Oct 18 so CardioHealth can run the eval queries and assess latency/cost.

Jonas — want to own the peering kickoff and the rotation doc? I'll set a standing 15-min checkpoint during the activation window.

Ben
Account Executive — Redwood Inference


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: netops@cardiohealth.com, Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@cardiohealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Oct 2026 09:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through

NetOps team / Liam — thanks. Quick technical plan and the minimal artifacts we'll need from CardioHealth to create the peering:
- Required from CardioHealth (NetOps): your VPC ID, the CIDR you will advertise, and a primary contact for route propagation acceptance.
- Required from Redwood: Redwood VPC ID (redwood-prod-eval-1), suggested routing (no default-route export), and a time window for BGP handshake.

Operational notes: 
- We'll provision an ephemeral API key scoped to the eval tenant with 72h TTL. It will be delivered through a signed Doc link (or HubSpot secure file) and rotated automatically after expiry.
- For the allowlist smoke test: provide a simple curl command we can run from our side to hit your staging endpoint once IPs are added. Sample: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <api-key>' https://staging.cardiohealth.com/v1/ping
- If CardioHealth prefers to initiate peering, we can share the Terraform snippet and the Redwood peer ASN. Otherwise, we will open the peering request and share the request id for tracking.

Proposed immediate next steps: 
1) Liam / NetOps confirm peering initiation preference and provide VPC ID (today).
2) CardioHealth open the suggested allowlist windows (if you want us to run smoke tests in the window, tell us the ETA).
3) Redwood publishes ephemeral key and sample curl for smoke (once org owner onboarding@cardiohealth.com is confirmed).

Can we do a 30-min call today at 3:00 PM PT to run through how we rotate keys and how we will validate routing? I will share a short runbook during the call.

Jonas Weber
Network & Integrations — Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: cardio-netops-peering-contacts.csv (CSV stub)


From: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@cardiohealth.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, netops@cardiohealth.com
Date: Mon, 18 Oct 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through

Jonas / team — we can initiate the VPC peering from CardioHealth side. NetOps will send the VPC ID and proposed routes by EOD (netops@cardiohealth.com). We also confirm the temporary org owner onboarding@cardiohealth.com.

3:00 PM PT today works; Jonas, please share the calendar invite (30 min) and the runbook link. We'll accept and have NetOps on the line.

Next steps checklist (per this thread):
- [CardioHealth] NetOps: send VPC ID + routing, open ephemeral allowlist window (EOD today).
- [Redwood] Jonas: share runbook + Terraform snippet; issue 72h ephemeral key to onboarding@cardiohealth.com after org is created.
- [Ben] schedule 15-min rapid checkpoint during activation window.

Thanks — looking forward to the call.

— Liam
Head of Integrations, CardioHealth Labs

title: Urgent: Aurora Bridge integration failures — action plan and SLA coordination
preview:
From: Asha Patel <asha@aurorabridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@aurorabridge.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Aurora Bridge webhook failures — immediate help needed

Hi Ben,

Quick summary: since ~08:45 PT this morning our integration with the Redwood hosted API has been intermittently failing — we see 504s and truncated responses when submitting webhook payloads from our ingestion pipeline. This is impacting a handful of production customers.

I attached trimmed logs showing repeated request IDs that time out (integration_logs_20261018.zip) and a short error trace we pulled (error_trace.txt). Our on-call (Tom Reynolds) is copied.

What we need from Redwood:
- An acknowledgement of the incident within 30 minutes (SLA expectation)
- A proposed mutual action plan (M.A.P.) and timeline for mitigation (quick rollback or routing change + permanent fix)
- Identify if this is a model/serving/regional capacity issue vs. a contract/rate-limit enforcement problem

We can be available on a quick bridge immediately if that helps — Tom and I are standing by. Please escalate to whoever owns partner infra/serving.

Thanks,

Asha Patel
Head of Integrations | Aurora Bridge
asha@aurorabridge.com
+1 415-555-0138

Attachments: integration_logs_20261018.zip (log bundle, 2.4 MB), error_trace.txt (snippet)


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Asha Patel <asha@aurorabridge.com>
Cc: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-18T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Aurora Bridge webhook failures — immediate help needed

Hi Asha — thanks for the heads up and for the attachments (I got the logs). Apologies for the disruption; I’m escalating this now to our on-call serving team and Partner Ops.

Immediate next steps from Redwood:
1) Acknowledge: We see the same 504 patterns in our ingress metrics and have flagged the requests from the request IDs you attached.
2) Bridge: I’m spinning up a Zoom bridge and propose a 10:30 PT sync with teams (I’ll invite a serving engineer and the partner manager).
3) MAP: Drafted below is a short mutual action plan we can execute today — please confirm availability: 
   - 10:30 PT: Conference to validate whether failures are caused by routing/throughput or by payload shapes triggering a model kernel path.
   - 11:00–12:30 PT: If routing, temporarily route Aurora traffic to an alternate pool with relaxed batching; if model, enable fallback to a lower-cap variant to reduce timeouts.
   - 14:00 PT: Post-mortem and follow-up owner assignments; potential config-only rollback scheduled if needed.

We consider the Slack incident severity at P2 until we confirm impact footprint; if customers continue to be impacted after the bridge, we’ll escalate to P1.

I’ve CC’d Sergio (Partner Ops) and Laura (Serving engineer) to lead the technical triage and to coordinate the SLA clause that applies to partner routing failures. Marissa (CS) — can you prep the customer impact list for Aurora customers we should notify?

Zoom: https://zoom.redwood.ai/j/682199034 (join at 10:30 PT)

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Partners | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 650-555-0199


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.ai>, Asha Patel <asha@aurorabridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-18T12:00:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Aurora Bridge webhook failures — immediate help needed

On Mon, Oct 18, 2026 at 10:05 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai> wrote:
> I’m spinning up a Zoom bridge and propose a 10:30 PT sync with teams

Thanks — quick technical note from triage: we identified repeated KV-cache thrashing correlated with certain long-payload webhook batches from Aurora. Two possible mitigations we can do today: 1) route Aurora to a dedicated fallback pool with a higher concurrency cap (fast, config-only); 2) enable request-level prefix caching for these endpoints (near-term patch, requires canary rollout).

Proposed technical plan (short):
- 12:15 PT: apply request-routing override to move Aurora traffic to fallback pool (0–15m risk window).
- 12:20 PT: monitor for reduced 504s; if still failing, roll back and try prefix-caching canary at 12:45 PT.
- 13:30 PT: revert routing override and apply long-term fix (performance tuning + QA) in a staged rollout.

I created a quick runbook reference: https://redwood.atlassian.net/l/c/aurora-integration-runbook (internal). I’ll attach the triage log snippet to the ticket shortly.

Attachment: triage_snippet.txt (included in ticket)

Laura Bennett
Staff Engineer, Serving Runtime | Redwood Inference
laura@redwood.ai


From: Asha Patel <asha@aurorabridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@aurorabridge.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-18T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Aurora Bridge webhook failures — immediate help needed

Ben/Laura/Sergio — thanks for the quick turnaround and clear plan. Tom and I joined the 10:30 bridge and saw the routing override succeed (fewer 504s observed for the last 4 hours). We’re comfortable with the staged plan you outlined.

Two follow-ups for the MAP and SLA coordination:
- Can Redwood provide written confirmation of the temporary routing override window and the targeted rollback times (we need this for our incident report and for compliance).
- Aurora would like an SLA amendment addendum that clarifies partner-specific routing fallback (we’re happy to have Partner Ops draft a short addendum referencing the mutual action plan today).

I’ve uploaded our incident summary to our shared drive and left a pointer in the HubSpot ticket: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/9481/company/aurora-bridge (see "Incident 2026-10-18").

We also attached the trimmed logs you asked for in the bridge (integration_logs_20261018.zip). If you need anything else, Tom and I are available tomorrow morning PT for a follow-up to finalize the addendum.

Best,

Asha Patel
Head of Integrations | Aurora Bridge
asha@aurorabridge.com
+1 415-555-0138

On Mon, Oct 18, 2026 at 12:02 PM Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai> wrote:
> I created a quick runbook reference: https://redwood.atlassian.net/l/c/aurora-integration-runbook (internal).

Attachments: none in this message (logs were previously uploaded).

title: Altura Financial DPA — retention carveout, evidence ask, and signature choreography
preview:
From: Samantha Price <samantha.price@alturafin.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Ruiz <tom.ruiz@alturafin.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 09:00:00 -0700
Subject: Altura DPA - initial redlines and retention question

Hi Ben / Lauren,

Thanks again for the demo last week — Altura is excited about the integration. Our legal team reviewed Redwood's master DPA and attached a marked-up version with a few requests:

- Retention: we asked for a 7-year retention carveout for transaction-level records tied to audit/AML obligations.
- Subprocessors: a more explicit subprocessor notification window (30 days).
- Data residency: we requested an EU-only option for a subset of records (see redline).

I've attached Altura_DPA_redlines.pdf so you can review the exact language. We also need a short SOC 2 evidence pack (last 12 months) and a completed SIG/CAIQ stub. Can you confirm what evidence you can provide?

Appreciate quick turnaround — our procurement team is trying to get this across the finish line next week.

Samantha
Samantha Price
Head of Procurement, Altura Financial
(attachment: Altura_DPA_redlines.pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Price <samantha.price@alturafin.com>

title: Embed store technical lab and resourcing request
preview:
From: Sonia Patel <sonia@datashed.io>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Liu <tom.liu@qdrant.ai>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Proposal: technical lab for S3 + pgvector fallthrough (Datashed x Redwood)

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for the intro last week — Datashed would love to run a 2-week technical lab with Redwood to validate a recommended fallthrough architecture for customers using pgvector + S3 cold lanes. High-level ask:

- Goal: reproduce realistic query fallthrough for 100k doc index, measure latency/cost delta across cold-tier strategies and cache warmup patterns.
- Redwood ask: 2 FTEs (1 infra engineer, 1 product eng) for pairing sessions + short integration work (est. 40 hours total).
- Deliverables: lab runbook, perf report, suggested default fallthrough config for Redwood Optimize.

Attached is a short scope doc with datasets and pass/fail criteria (datashed_lab_scope.pdf). Proposed start: week of Nov 8.

We can cover co-marketing (customer study + joint blog) if lab completes. Would love to know resource availability and any compliance/credential steps we need for S3 access.

Best,
Sonia Patel
Partnerships, Datashed

Attachment: datashed_lab_scope.pdf (pdf, 320KB)

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sonia Patel <sonia@datashed.io>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: technical lab for S3 + pgvector fallthrough (Datashed x Redwood)

Sonia — thanks, this looks useful and aligned with our partner program goals.

Quick answers:
- I can allocate Ben for the infra pairing (experiments + perf harness) and Vanessa can support the GTM/case study side. I'll cover product coordination.
- Credentials: we prefer a short-lived service account with limited S3 scope (put/get on a test bucket). Please send your IP allowlist if you have static egress.
- Timeline: Nov 8 start works on our side; can we lock a 90-min kickoff on Nov 9 to align datasets and success criteria? I put a placeholder on my calendar: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abcd1234

Next steps:
1) Sonia — send us the sample dataset + any access instructions.
2) Ben — I'll add you to the drive and drop the initial runbook.

Onward — Avery

Vanessa — if lab completes, Datashed wants a joint case study and a short webinar. Can you scope the GTM commitment (1 PM + 1 field enablement rep ~ 20 hours)? We can trade marketing credit if we need to compensate.

Subject: Re: Proposal: technical lab for S3 + pgvector fallthrough (Datashed x Redwood)

Confirmed: Datashed will provision the sample dataset and a test S3 bucket (read/write creds for a short-lived role). I've attached access instructions and a checklist for the kickoff: lab-access-instructions.txt. We'll plan the kickoff for Nov 9, 10:00am PT (I accepted the calendar invite).

preview:

Cc: security@medixis.com, procurement@medixis.com
Date: 2026-10-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request: 12m log retention carveout + CAIQ evidence

Hi Ben,

Thanks for taking this — Medixis has a compliance requirement to retain certain inference and audit logs for 12 months for a specific clinical workflow (patient-match and de-identification flows). Our baseline review of Redwood's standard TTL (90 days) flags a gap for that workload.

Can Redwood confirm whether we can: 
- Preserve tenant-scoped request & audit logs for a 12-month window for a designated tenant ID (Medixis-prod)
- Provide the CAIQ / SIG mappings and a SOC 2 artifact showing the retention policy and proof-of-delete for expired samples

We are prepared to sign a limited addendum if needed; procurement copied. For context, the log types are: request headers, inference trace (no patient PHI stored), and system audit trails. I attached a short request packet.

Attachments: Medixis_Retention_Request.pdf (request form)

Thanks,
Claire Jensen
Director of Security Engineering
Medixis Health

- A CAIQ export and SIG matrix scoped to the Terraform/runtime controls that govern log retention and deletion.
- SOC 2 evidence: retention policy excerpt and documented proof-of-delete workflows (sample audit) — we'll include a proof-of-delete run for a non-production tenant for verification.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive — Redwood Inference

title: Commercial proposal and rate schedule — Novatech RFP
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Sharma <procurement@novatechsys.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Commercial proposal and rate schedule — Novatech RFP

Hi Priya,

Thanks for sharing the RFP. Attaching an initial commercial package focused on predictable unit economics and simple reconciliation: a short rate schedule (CSV) and a one-page summary. Highlights below so your procurement and finance teams can scan quickly: 

- Volume anchor (annual): a preferred annual usage anchor that sets an effective blended price. Anchor is optional — we show example math for both anchor and flexible monthly plans.
- Month-to-month tiers: incremental price bands as usage grows with an automatic smoothing window to avoid short spikes increasing unit price.
- Burst buffer: a 20% monthly buffer for transient peaks; sustained burst usage (beyond 72 hours) converts to tiered billing.
- Overage cap protection: overage rates are capped at 1.5x list for the first 60 days after onboarding, then step-down to regular tier rates thereafter.
- Billing & reconciliation: monthly invoices + quarterly reconciliation (example calculations included).

Attached: Novatech_RFP_v3.pdf (copy of your RFP we used), Redwood_NOVATECH_rate_examples.xlsx (worked examples).

If it helps, I can walk your team through two scenarios on a quick call: 1) annual anchor with stepped discount ladder, 2) flexible monthly with burst smoothing. What time works next week?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0142

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Sharma <procurement@novatechsys.com>
Date: 2026-10-14T08:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Commercial proposal and rate schedule — Novatech RFP

Ben / Marissa / Priya — short pricing note and examples below. I attached a worked spreadsheet (same workbook we shared) with formula cells.

Principles: keep math transparent, protect Novatech from spike pricing, and provide ramp protections during onboarding.

Pricing primitives (list prices and examples):
- Generation token list price: $0.50 per 1M tokens (base list)
- Embedding token list price: $0.08 per 1M tokens (base list)
- Volume anchor discount ladder (annual anchored commitment): 1) 0-3M/mo = 8% off list; 2) 3-10M/mo = 15% off list; 3) >10M/mo = 22% off list
- Flexible monthly plan: apply tiered per-month pricing with smoothing window = 30 days; effective banded pricing reduces volatility (examples in tab "FlexibleMonthly").
- Burst buffer: 20% of prior-month average as an allowance. Trigger rule = sliding 72-hour window that exceeds the allowance and whose sustained level is maintained for 72 consecutive hours. Once triggered, excess usage is billed at the next higher tier but with an introductory cap: 1.5x list for first two billing cycles post-trigger.
- Repeated overages: if a customer triggers the burst >3 months in a rolling 6-month period, we automatically recommend moving to the annual anchor for a blended lower rate or add a negotiated sustained tier; we do not auto-raise rates on an existing signed term without discussion.

Worked examples (effective per-1M-token prices):
- Scenario A (Anchor, example 6M/mo): list $0.50, anchor ladder gives blended ~ $0.39 per 1M tokens (after 22% top-band on majority of volume) — see sheet for cell-level math.
- Scenario B (Flexible 2M/mo with one 3-day spike): flexible banding yields ~ $0.44 per 1M tokens after smoothing; burst charged at 1.5x list for the first two cycles on excess, then step down to tiered rate.

Billing cadence & reconciliation: monthly invoicing (Net 30) + quarterly reconciliation where we true the effective blended price vs paid invoices and issue a credit or debit on the next quarter's invoice. For anchor customers we provide a simple amortization schedule so there are no surprise over/under charges.

Usage evidence & audit: we provide line-item CSV exports with hourly model and token totals plus a signed usage statement on request. For formal audits we offer read-only access to the usage portal plus an annual signed attestation.

Attachments: Redwood_NOVATECH_rate_examples.xlsx (scenarios), pricing_formula_notes.txt (explanations).

Happy to hop on a 30-minute call to walk the finance team through the spreadsheet.

—Karthik
Staff Sales Engineer — Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai | +1 617-555-0123


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>

Date: 2026-10-16T14:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Commercial proposal and rate schedule — Novatech RFP (legal summary)

Ben / Team / Priya,

Attached is a short legal summary and proposed redlines for the commercial term sheet. High-level bullets: 

- Billing terms: monthly invoices, Net 30. Quarterly reconciliation; for annual anchors, an amortized schedule will be appended to the SOW.
- Audit & usage evidence: Redwood will provide hourly usage CSVs, model identifiers, and a signed usage summary for each reconciliation period. For deeper audits, Novatech may request one annual audit (with 30 days' advance notice); reasonable audit costs borne by Novatech unless a material discrepancy (>3%) is found, in which case Redwood bears verified audit costs.
- SLA and credits: propose 99.5% monthly availability target for API endpoints used by Novatech. Credit ladder: 5% of monthly invoice for <99.0% but >=98.0%; 10% for <98%; credit calculation capped at 50% monthly invoice. Credits are the exclusive remedy for availability failures.
- Data residency & retention: Redwood will process Novatech data in US regions by default; we can offer EU-only processing for an additional compliance addendum. Retention defaults to 180 days with customer-configurable KV cache lifetimes.
- Overages & remedies: the 1.5x introductory overage cap is acceptable for onboarding. For recurring sustained overages, Redwood will propose a negotiated sustained tier and provide 60 days' notice before changing pricing for future invoices. No retroactive price increases on previously billed usage.
- Termination & exit: include an export deliverable clause (usage extracts + model artifacts where applicable) and a 60-day transition window.

I have attached redline suggestions in legal_summary_redlines.docx and inline comments in the Drive link. Ben — please confirm if you want me to (a) apply these language changes to the provided commercial term sheet and return a single PDF for Priya, or (b) if you prefer to run a quick sync with their legal counsel first.

Marissa Cole

Ben / Marissa / Priya — short pricing note and examples below. I attached a worked spreadsheet (same workbook we shared) with formula cells.

Principles: keep math transparent, protect Novatech from spike pricing, and provide ramp protections during onboarding.

Pricing primitives (list prices and examples):
- Generation token list price: $0.50 per 1M tokens (base list)
- Embedding token list price: $0.08 per 1M tokens (base list)
- Volume anchor discount ladder (annual anchored commitment): 1) 0-3M/mo = 8% off list; 2) 3-10M/mo = 15% off list; 3) >10M/mo = 22% off list
- Flexible monthly plan: apply tiered per-month pricing with smoothing window = 30 days; effective banded pricing reduces volatility (examples in tab "FlexibleMonthly").
- Burst buffer: 20% of prior-month average as an allowance. Trigger rule = sliding 72-hour window that exceeds the allowance and whose sustained level is maintained for 72 consecutive hours. Once triggered, excess usage is billed at the next higher tier but with an introductory cap: 1.5x list for first two billing cycles post-trigger.
- Repeated overages: if a customer triggers the burst >3 months in a rolling 6-month period, we automatically recommend moving to the annual anchor for a blended lower rate or add a negotiated sustained tier; we do not auto-raise rates on an existing signed term without discussion.

Worked examples (effective per-1M-token prices):
- Scenario A (Anchor, example 6M/mo): list $0.50, anchor ladder gives blended ~ $0.39 per 1M tokens (after 22% top-band on majority of volume) — see sheet for cell-level math.
- Scenario B (Flexible 2M/mo with one 3-day spike): flexible banding yields ~ $0.44 per 1M tokens after smoothing; burst charged at 1.5x list for the first two cycles on excess, then step down to tiered rate.

Billing cadence & reconciliation: monthly invoicing (Net 30) + quarterly reconciliation where we true the effective blended price vs paid invoices and issue a credit or debit on the next quarter's invoice. For anchor customers we provide a simple amortization schedule so there are no surprise over/under charges.

Usage evidence & audit: we provide line-item CSV exports with hourly model and token totals plus a signed usage statement on request. For formal audits we offer read-only access to the usage portal plus an annual signed attestation.

title: SOC 2 Type II — initial intake and suggested share + quick reply templates
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Emma Rios <emma@orbitapps.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Oct 2026 16:12:00 +0000
Subject: SOC 2 Type II — initial intake and suggested share + quick reply templates

Hi Emma —

Thanks for taking this on. For Orbit Apps to complete our procurement and security review we typically request the following from vendors who have a SOC 2 Type II: a copy of the final report (PDF), a short bridge letter if the report period doesn't cover our desired timeframe, and a small set of supporting artifacts to validate controls.

To make this low-friction, I've attached an \"ask matrix\" (CSV) with checkboxes you can use — but below is a concise prioritized list you can reply with or drop into a secure share. If you prefer an access-only model (report viewer), that works too — details below.

Audit coverage we usually expect: the report period (audit start/end) and whether the report is Type II. If you have an overlap gap with our target period we accept a bridge letter that confirms no material control changes since the report date.

Priority items (what to share first):
- SOC 2 Type II report (PDF) — required
- Bridge letter / management assertion (one page) if audit period < desired window — strongly preferred
- Control narratives or system descriptions for in-scope services (doc or link)
- Evidence samples (redacted): change management ticket snapshot, access review output, backup success logs for a recent date within the audit window
- List of in-scope subprocessors and where telemetrics/logs are stored (SaaS/cloud region)
- Authentication & SSO configuration summary, and retention policy excerpt

Optional but helpful:
- Pen test summary (if available)
- Incident response runbook excerpt and most recent tabletop date

Secure transfer options we support: S3 presigned upload, one-time SharePoint/Drive folder with expiry, or SFTP dropbox. If you'd like, we can provide a short DocuSign or secure portal link.

Quick reply templates (copy/paste) — use these for legal/procurement: 

1) Procurement short reply (if you can share immediately):
\"Hi Ben/Grace — attaching our SOC 2 Type II report (period: 2025-09-01 to 2026-08-31) and a short bridge letter confirming no material control changes since the report. Also included: access review sample and control narratives. Link: <secure-presigned-url>. Thanks, Emma\"

2) Legal redaction request (if you need to limit PII):
\"Hi Ben — we can provide a redacted SOC 2 Type II and a bridge letter. For detailed log samples we will redact customer identifiers; please confirm if tokenized samples are acceptable. — Emma\"

3) Access-only option:
\"Hi — we can grant temporary viewer access to our compliance portal (30 days). Please confirm contact emails for access provisioning. — Emma\"

Attachments: redwood-soc2-ask-matrix.csv (asks + examples), sample-bridge-letter.txt, secure-share-options.md

If you want, Grace/Naomi and I can draft a short one-line procurement acceptance template for Orbit's legal team that they can paste into their approval email.

Next step: can you confirm whether you can share the report and bridge letter this week or need a 1–2 week window? Also let me know which secure transfer method you prefer.

Appreciate it —
Ben

Ben Carter
Enterprise Security & Trust, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0142


From: Emma Rios <emma@orbitapps.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Orbit Apps Legal <legal@orbitapps.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Oct 2026 09:47:00 +0000
Subject: Re: SOC 2 Type II — initial intake and suggested share + quick reply templates

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clear matrix. A couple quick clarifications from Legal/IT before we share: 

1) Audit window: Our most recent Type II covers 2025-10-01 to 2026-09-30. You requested 2025-09-01 to 2026-08-31 — is the slight offset okay or do you need exact alignment?
2) Evidence samples: for logs and backups we usually provide tokenized/redacted examples. Is that acceptable as long as timestamps and control-state are intact?
3) Subprocessor list: we keep this updated but sometimes it's a bundled vendor list — ok to provide as-is?

We can do S3 presigned upload — legal will attach a short bridge letter if you confirm the acceptable wording. If you have a one-line procurement approval we can paste it into our ticket to speed sign-off.

Thanks —
Emma Rios
Head of IT Security, Orbit Apps
emma@orbitapps.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Emma Rios <emma@orbitapps.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Oct 2026 13:05:00 +0000
Subject: Re: SOC 2 Type II — initial intake and suggested share + quick reply templates

Emma — this looks great, thanks. Short answers inline: 

1) Audit window: small offsets are fine. We mainly need confirmation that the controls in the report represent the same service configuration during your audit period. If you can provide a bridge letter that says \"no material control changes affecting in-scope services between 2025-09-01 and 2026-09-30\" that will cover our legal/infosec ask.

2) Redacted/tokenized logs: perfectly acceptable. We need identifiable control artifacts (e.g., admin user creation event, backup success entry) with timestamps preserved. Redact customer identifiers but keep the event metadata.

3) Subprocessor list: yes, provide the bundled list — we don't need separate contracts, just names and purpose.

Below are two short one-line procurement/legal acceptance templates you can use verbatim: 

Procurement affirmative (for quick approvals):
\"Orbit Apps confirms receipt of the SOC 2 Type II report and bridge letter; findings reviewed and sufficient for purchase approval. — Orbit Apps Procurement\"

Legal redaction assurance (to accompany redacted artifacts):
\"The provided artifacts are redacted to protect customer PII. Redactions are limited to identifiers; control-state, timestamps, and event types are unmodified. — Orbit Apps Legal\"

If you'd like, Karthik (cc'd) can accept the report on our side and move this forward with procurement once the uploads arrive. I'll also add a 30-day viewer option to the ask matrix so you can choose that instead of file upload.

Thanks again —
Ben

Hi team,

We have the Outreach renewal approaching (term ends 11/30) and I wanted to propose a slightly different approach than a straight seat license renewal. Summary of proposal: 

- Purchase a base committed seat pool for 120 users and a 20-seat flex buffer (seat credits that can be reassigned monthly).
- Add a 6-month enablement retainer (SDR bootcamp + playbook implementation, up to 10 days of vendor trainer time).
- Net effect should reduce churn overhead and speed ramp for new hires; conservative cost estimate attached.

Attachments: Outreach_Quote_20261012.xlsx, SOW_SDREnablement_v1.pdf, CostComparison_EngagementTools.csv

Question for procurement/finance: can we route part of the retainer to FY27 professional services (to align with hiring plan) and treat seat flex as subscription? Ben, I can draft allocation if you confirm the fund code.

Why this hybrid: our SDR headcount is projected to fluctuate with Q1 hiring; having a small flex pool avoids multiple seat reassignments and refresh tickets. The retainer keeps the vendors responsible for a faster completion of training and DB of playbooks.

Next steps I propose:
1) Procurement to sign off on allocation and PO path (internal)
2) Ask Jordan (Outreach) for a DocuSign-ready quote with net-30 invoice terms and explicit seat-flex mechanics
3) Legal to run the SOW and confirm standard data security annex

Ping me if you'd prefer Salesloft out of the gate instead — I did include a cost row in the comparison but Outreach has the better implementation bundle right now.

Alex Martinez

title: MSA / Order Form — staggered payment ladder, invoice recipient & VAT allocation
preview:
From: Emma Saunders <emma@starlite-ops.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@starlite-eu.com>, finance@starlite-eu.com
Date: Tue, 12 Oct 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Order flow questions — payment ladder, invoice recipient, VAT

Hi Alex,

Thanks for sending over the draft order form. A few quick questions from our side to make sure procurement & Treasury can wire things correctly:

1) Payment cadence: Starlite prefers staged invoices tied to delivery milestones. You proposed three tranches over 12 months — can you confirm the invoice dates align to usage start rather than signature date? We need dates for cashflow planning.

2) Invoice recipient & legal entity: We need the invoice sent to Starlite Technologies BV (EU entity) for VAT purposes. Is Redwood able to provide entity-level invoicing (US entity for the upfront, EU for recurring)?

3) VAT liability: For our EU entity we typically apply reverse charge where applicable. Will Redwood accept reverse charge on recurring tokens, or do you require us to provide VAT registration evidence first?

I attached our PO so you can see the internal reference. Once we confirm, I can route a formal PO and start the DocuSign flow.

Appreciate a quick sync — our CFO would like this decided before the month-end.

Best,
Emma Saunders
Head of Procurement
Starlite Technologies

Attachment: PO_Starlite_20261010.pdf (application/pdf, 115KB)

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Emma Saunders <emma@starlite-ops.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Oct 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Order flow questions — payment ladder, invoice recipient, VAT

Hi Emma — thanks for the clear note and the PO. Looping in Ben (sales lead) and Kimberly (legal) so we can align on the order form redlines.

Short answers up front:
- Payment dates: we can tie invoice triggers to "start of use" milestones. For the three-tranche model I recommend: invoice 1 at signature (upfront onboarding credit), invoice 2 at 30 days post-start, invoice 3 at 180 days post-start. If you need fixed calendar dates instead we can map those in the order form.
- Entity-level invoicing: we can accept split invoicing (US entity for upfront, Redwood EU subsidiary for recurrent). Need a legal name and remit-to address for the EU entity on the order form.
- VAT / reverse charge: we routinely accept reverse charge where applicable, but we require VAT evidence (VAT number + certificate) prior to applying reverse-charge treatment on recurring invoices. Kimberly — can you confirm the clause & the evidence checklist?

I've uploaded a redline of the order form with placeholders for remit-to and the tranche triggers: Starlite_OrderForm_redline.docx (attached). Please review and flag any objections.

Next steps I propose:
1) Emma to confirm remit-to entity name and VAT number.
2) Kimberly to provide the specific evidence items we require (short checklist).
3) Ben & I to update billing triggers in the OF and start DocuSign once redlines are accepted.

Thanks — happy to jump on a 20m call if helpful.

— Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Starlite_OrderForm_redline.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, 48KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Emma Saunders <emma@starlite-ops.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>

title: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-10-11 09:04 PDT
Subject: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Team — kicking this off. High level and asks below; aim is to lock targets and a baseline scenario by the end of week 3 (Oct 29). Attaching the draft timeline and the scenario assumptions doc.

Why now
- We need a single set of anchors for FY27 targets before the product roadmap decouples from capacity assumptions.
- QBR deck needs to be draft-ready for exec review the week of Nov 8.

Primary asks (owners in parens)
1) Propose 3-target bands (Aggressive / Base / Conserv.) for ARR, Net Retention, and Hosting unit cost by Oct 22 (Marcus).
2) Define capacity and hiring assumptions that materially change cost-per-token (Laura + Jonas) — call out any procurement lead times.
3) Draft A/B scenario narrative for a 20% demand shock and a 35% latency-sensitive adoption (Grace).
4) Book a 1.5h cross-functional planning workshop (calendar invite will follow) and a separate 30m QBR dry-run for leadership (Ava).

Attachments: [Attachment: 2026_planning_timeline.xlsx (spreadsheet)], [Attachment: scenario_assumptions_v1.md (text)]

Quick timeline
- Oct 22: targets & assumptions due
- Oct 29: consolidated scenario and risk register
- Nov 2: pre-read to execs
- Nov 8: leadership QBR review

Notes and constraints to consider
- We should bake in a conservative buffer for Dedicated capacity lead times (6–8 weeks) and a second-tier fallback model (smaller quantized variant) for latency-critical customers.
- Call out any SLAs that would require SLA credits or changes to pricing.

I know this is busy — if you prefer to split deliverables and own fewer items, flag it in your reply.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Head of Product, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
[Attachment: 2026_planning_timeline.xlsx]


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-11 10:18 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Thanks — good framing. A few clarifying Qs and an initial ask list below.

Clarifying Qs
- For \"unit cost\" do you want per-1k-token blended cost at 95th percentile latency or average cost? I'd recommend 95th.
- Are we assuming current quantization profiles or applying the Optimize recommendations?

Immediate deliverables (Marcus)
- I can roughly sketch target bands by Oct 18 if we assume current profiles, or Oct 22 if we incorporate Optimize changes (I prefer Oct 22 to keep it accurate).
- Preliminary numbers: Aggressive = +55% ARR growth target, Base = +35%, Conserv. = +15% (ballpark — will refine).

Blocking items
- Need a clear headcount plan from infra/procurement for dedicated pools — Laura, can you confirm hiring + procurement lead times this week?

I'll start filling the number sheet and flag assumptions inline.

—Marcus
 Marcus Lin
 Head of Revenue Ops
[Sent from my laptop]

To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-11 15:02 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Ava / Marcus — quick status from capacity/procurement side.

- Hiring: realistic ramp for infra engineers + ops is +8 FTEs spread across Q1. Recruit + offer cadence suggests start-of-hire in mid-Nov; impact on capacity by late-Q1.
- Dedicated GPU lead time: 6–9 weeks for procurement -> install in cloud partner (conservative is 9 weeks if special hardware).
- Cost note: GPU price decline assumptions in scenario_assumptions_v1.md are optimistic; recommend a Base that assumes a modest 10% unit price drop vs. the optimistic 25%.

Attached a short note with procurement milestones and a quick headcount impact table.

[Attachment: procurement_headcount_note.pdf (doc stub)]

VP, Platform Operations

Ben / Kimberly —

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday. We are proceeding with the CAIQ review for Acme Retail and need two items to close the "Runtime Isolation" control family:

1) A compact control map showing which Redwood services run in multi-tenant vs dedicated pools, and which controls are enforced at the orchestration layer vs at the VM/gpu runtime. A single-page diagram or CSV crosswalk is ideal.

2) Any current exceptions or planned mitigations for cross-tenant memory-residency (if applicable). If you have a short attestation from the runtime team on expected remediation schedule, that will help Acme's risk committee.

Can you provide the artifacts by EOD Oct 13 PT? If not, propose the earliest delivery. Also attach any sample access logs that demonstrate tenant separation enforcement for the last 30 days.

Thanks — Samira
Lead Auditor, BlueCrest Audit

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@bluecrest-audit.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Evan Lee <evan.lee@acmeretail.com>
Date: 2026-10-12 14:20 PDT
Subject: Re: CAIQ follow-up: runtime isolation mapping and exceptions request

Samira —

Short update from the runtime team: we prepared a compact crosswalk and a single-page diagram that shows the service routing and enforcement points. High level notes below and attached files:

- Format: attached PDF for visual diagram + CSV index (caiq-attachment-index.csv) for control mappings to our internal control IDs.
- Multi-tenant vs dedicated: generation, batching, and KV cache processes are multi-tenant by default; customer-dedicated pools are used for Dedicated and Private deployments. Control enforcement for isolation is split between the orchestration layer (namespace & runtime policy) and the gpu runtime (memory guards and periodic scrubs).
- Exceptions: We have one open exception where a legacy caching path could allow ephemeral cross-tenant cache persistence for sequences > 64k tokens; mitigation is scheduled for 2026-11-05 and is tracked in RFC-7543. Jonas will provide attestation language.

Attached: runtime-isolation-diagram.pdf (pdf), caiq-attachment-index.csv (csv), access-log-excerpt.txt (txt)

Kimberly Park
Security Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@bluecrest-audit.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Evan Lee <evan.lee@acmeretail.com>
Date: 2026-10-13 15:04 PDT
Subject: Re: CAIQ follow-up: runtime isolation mapping and exceptions request

Samira —

Attestation and remediation timeline below. Please see the attached attestation snippet (pasted) and the RFC reference for the change window. If Acme requires a formal signed attestation, we can route through our compliance portal — let me know.

Attestation (draft):
"Redwood Inference maintains logical and physical runtime isolation controls between tenants. We have identified a legacy caching path that, under a specific sequence length and batching pattern, could result in ephemeral cross-tenant cache residue. The engineering team has scheduled a remediation for 2026-11-05; mitigations include an aggressive cache eviction policy and a runtime scrub during cold-starts. During the remediation window, runtime logs and the observability pipeline will be used to detect and alert on anomalous cache hits."

Remediation RFC: RFC-7543 (internal) — fix ETA 2026-11-05
Action items:
- Ben: confirm Acme is OK with the remediation ETA and whether they prefer a signed attestation (DocuSign).
- Priya: push the evidence bundle to the Acme secure Share (Drive link) and mark items 1-3 as complete when uploaded.

Jonas Weber
Senior Software Engineer, Runtime — Redwood Inference

title: Pricing sensitivity workbook & next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Nguyen <cnguyen@novaterra.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing sensitivity workbook & next steps

Hi Claire —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our discovery:

- Use case: Novaterra search + summarization for support docs (hybrid RAG + conversational UI).
- Current pilot traffic expectation: ~12M prompt tokens / month and ~48M completion tokens / month (total ~60M tokens).
- Key SLOs: p95 latency < 400ms for short-turn queries, retention of KV cache for multi-turn sessions.
- Primary ask: budget range and target unit cost ($/1M tokens) that would make you comfortable moving from pilot to reserved capacity.

Next steps I proposed on the call:
1) I’ll send a pricing sensitivity workbook with 3 scenarios (pilot, scale, reserved/committed) showing unit economics, monthly invoice-style estimate, and an attachment-friendly breakdown.
2) You confirm the budget band (we discussed $40k–$120k/mo range as a starting point).
3) If the numbers look good, we prepare a short 6-month pilot commit + reserved option and loop in procurement/legal.

If that matches your notes, let me know and I’ll share the workbook and a one-page invoice estimate by EOD.

Cheers,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0172

Attachments: none in this message

To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Novaterra Procurement <procurement@novaterra.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 18:09:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Pricing sensitivity workbook & next steps

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the recap — that aligns with my notes. A few clarifying points before you send the workbook:

- Can you show the numbers as $/1M tokens and the total monthly invoice line? We’ll need that for our internal finance review.
- Our target unit cost to hit procurement approval is roughly $700–$850 per 1M tokens (depending on SLA and retention). Can you include scenarios that hit those bands?
- If we consider reserved capacity (3–6 month commit), what bucket sizes and discount tiers do you recommend?
- Also include: any additional line items (e.g., private deployment management, enhanced retention, audit logging) with per-month adders so we can model TCO.

If you can attach an invoice-style PDF and the workbook with the calculator, I’ll circulate to procurement and finance.

Claire Nguyen
Head of AI Product | Novaterra
claire.nguyen@novaterra.ai

On Mon, Oct 12, 2026 at 3:02 PM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Claire —
> 
> Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our discovery: ...


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Nguyen <cnguyen@novaterra.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing workbook + invoice estimate (attachments)

Claire —

Appended are the attachments we discussed: novaterra_pricing_sensitivity_20261012.xlsx (workbook with three scenarios), redwood_pricing_calculator_v2.xlsx (calculator with token unit-cost sliders), and estimate_novaterra_20261012.pdf (one-page invoice-style estimate). Key highlights below so procurement/finance can scan quickly:

Summary scenarios (all numbers shown as $ per 1M tokens and total monthly):

1) Pilot (no commit) — conservative usage: 60M tokens / month
   - Unit price: $800 per 1M tokens
   - Monthly cost: 60 * $800 = $48,000
   - Notes: Hosted API, standard SLA, autoscaling; predictable but higher unit cost.

2) Scale (partial commit) — expected growth: 120M tokens / month
   - Unit price: $640 per 1M tokens (10% volume discounts staged across bands)
   - Monthly cost: 120 * $640 = $76,800
   - Notes: 3-month rolling commit, lightweight KV cache optimizations included.

3) Reserved (committed capacity) — heavy usage: 180M tokens / month
   - Unit price: $450 per 1M tokens (reserved pool + quantization/steady-state batching)
   - Monthly cost: 180 * $450 = $81,000
   - Notes: Dedicated pool, SLA-backed latency targets, lower unit economics. Also includes a 15% prepay credit option to reduce cashflow friction.

Attachments include a per-feature breakout (generation vs embeddings vs reranking), expected token mix assumptions (prompt vs completion ratios), and an expected margin/COGS line to illustrate Redwood economics at each price point. The workbook also has a slider for your target $/1M tokens so you can see required monthly volume to hit a specific unit cost.

Requested next steps:
- Claire / procurement: please confirm which scenario(s) you want formalized (pilot vs committed). If reserved, confirm desired commit term (3 or 6 months) and whether you prefer prepay.
- If you want, we can convert scenario 3 into a straight line-item proposal and a draft SOW for legal to review.

I’ve cc’d Marissa and Rishi to help on margin/discount approvals and to prepare a short contract draft if you want to move forward.

Thanks —
Ben
Attachments: novaterra_pricing_sensitivity_20261012.xlsx (Excel, 78 KB); redwood_pricing_calculator_v2.xlsx (Excel, 220 KB); estimate_novaterra_20261012.pdf (PDF, 45 KB)


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 16:18:00 -0400
Subject: Internal: Novaterra pricing path + recommended concession

Folks — quick internal note (threading off the Novaterra thread):

- Customer target band: $700–$850 / 1M tokens. Our model shows we can hit $700 at ~140–160M tokens with a modest reserved discount; $650 requires heavier commit or additional optimizations (quantization + model variant).
- I recommended to Claire a 6-month reserved pilot at $450 / 1M for 180M tokens (option to prepay 15% for better cash terms). She asked for invoice and workbook, which I attached.
- Ask: can finance/legal approve a 6-month reserved pilot with the following concessions?
   1) 15% prepay credit (applied to first invoice)
   2) Standard enterprise SLA with 99.9% availability for the dedicated pool
   3) A 30-day termination window after month 3 if usage remains >40% under commit (mitigates their risk)

If you are comfortable with those items, I’ll push the committed scenario to procurement and ask them to run legal review. Also looping in Marissa for any additional margin constraints.

Thanks —
Ben

title: Evidence export: credentialed split delivery plan
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 9 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Evidence export: credentialed split delivery plan

Hi Jordan,

Thanks for the SOC 2 evidence request. To make the delivery as low-friction as possible for your audit team, Redwood can do a two-stage export: 1) a signed metadata/manifest (file list + checksums + HMAC) and 2) the bulk admin-activity bundles. A few quick questions so we can size and schedule the job: 

- Preferred transport: TLS SFTP with a scoped user, or S3 presigned URLs?
- Scope: rolling 90 days? Or a fixed window (start/end timestamps)?
- Granularity: do you need tenant-separated bundles (separate file per tenant) or combined with tenant ID in records?
- Compliance ask: do you require HMAC-signed manifest and SHA-256 checksums?

We can provide a brief runbook and a sample manifest schema for validation before the transfer. If you want, we can do an initial 7-day validation export to confirm checksums and the transfer process.

Proposed timeline: once transport and scope are agreed, we can schedule an initial validation export within 48 hours and full transfer within your requested window.

Looping in Kim (tech lead) to confirm the runbook and manifest layout.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: export_runbook_draft.md (draft), sample_manifest_schema.json (schema stub)

From: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@acmecloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 9 Oct 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Evidence export: credentialed split delivery plan

Ben — thanks for the quick reply. Answers below: 

- Transport: our security team prefers TLS SFTP with a dedicated account and key-based auth. S3 presigned is acceptable as fallback but needs to be one-time URLs and encrypted in transit.
- Scope: full 90 days historical window (2026-07-11 — 2026-10-09) for admin activity.
- Granularity: we need tenant-separated bundles (one archive per tenant) plus a combined index manifest.
- Signing/checksums: we require SHA-256 checksums for each bundle and an HMAC-signed manifest. Please include a manifest signature method description.

title: EU-only processing and deletion evidence request
preview:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-10T09:12:00Z
Subject: EU-only processing confirmation + deletion evidence request

Hi Ava,

Thanks for taking this — Novamed's privacy & security team reviewed the materials from your team and we have a couple of follow-ups specific to our EU-only processing requirement (Schrems II / SCCs scope). Can you please confirm the following for our account:

1) Is production inference, backups, and snapshot storage kept physically within the EU region(s) (we'd prefer eu-central-1 / eu-west-1 only)?
2) Does the control plane (auth / metadata / job orchestration) store any cleartext identifiers or payload outside the EU for our account? If yes, what pseudonymization / encryption controls are applied and where are keys held?
3) Retention: what are the default retention windows for inference inputs, transient KV caches, model state snapshots, and audit logs?
4) Deletion proof: what artifacts can Redwood provide to prove data erasure (deletion manifests, snapshot IDs, audit log entries, checksums)?
5) Operator access: please describe conditions for human operator access to EU-hosted data and whether operator access is restricted to EU-based staff / controlled bastion.

We attached our security questionnaire (Novamed_Security_Questions_v2.pdf). This is time-sensitive — ideally we have a written response + evidence bundle within five business days and a short call to review.

Best,
Elena Ruiz
Head of Privacy, Novamed
elena.ruiz@novamed.com

Attachment: Novamed_Security_Questions_v2.pdf (pdf, 48KB)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-10T10:45:00Z
Subject: Re: EU-only processing confirmation + deletion evidence request

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear list. I'm looping in Naomi (privacy lead) and Jordan (ops) who will put together the evidence set.

Quick plan:
- Naomi will provide a written technical response outlining residency, control plane behavior, and operator access controls.
- Jordan will prepare a sample deletion manifest and the set of audit artifacts we can produce (hashes, timestamps, snapshot IDs).
- We can share an SCCs addendum and operator affidavit for Novamed to review.
- If helpful, we can schedule a 30-minute call tomorrow or Friday to walk through the artifacts.

Can you confirm: do you want the evidence bundle packaged as a single ZIP and uploaded to a secure share, or would you prefer separate docs uploaded to DocuSign/SharePoint? Also let us know any specific format constraints for deletion proof (CSV manifest, signed JSON, etc.).

We aim to get the first pass to you within 3 business days and the full evidence bundle within 5.

Thanks,

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-11T14:30:00Z
Subject: Re: EU-only processing confirmation + deletion evidence request

Elena — thanks for the questions. Below is a concise, account-scoped response for Novamed; I've focused on the controls and concrete artifacts we can provide. Note: these controls are enabled for Novamed's Dedicated EU-only deployment we provisioned during onboarding. If you were discussing a multi-tenant hosted plan, some control-plane metadata would follow a different path — we confirmed Dedicated EU-only is in place for your tenancy.

1) Physical residency
- Compute and persistent volumes: all inference compute, model caches, and persistent snapshots for Novamed are provisioned in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) and replicated only to eu-west-1 for cross-az redundancy; no replicas are placed outside the EU.
- Backups & snapshots: on a retention cycle configured for your tenancy (default 7 days for hourly snapshots, 90-day cyclic backup overwrite policy) — all stored in EU S3 buckets under our EU data partition.

2) Control plane behavior and keys
- Control plane: for Dedicated EU-only customers we run a private control plane within your VPC in the EU. Metadata that might include request identifiers is pseudonymized at ingestion and encrypted at rest with a customer-scoped CMK (if you provide one) or our EU-resident CMK by default. No cleartext inference payload leaves the EU.
- Key management: option to bring-your-own-key (BYOK) in an EU KMS (customer-managed) or use Redwood-managed EU CMK. Key operations for BYOK remain in EU.

3) Retention windows (defaults, can be tightened per contract)
- Inference inputs (raw request bodies): ephemeral by default, 24 hours unless flagged for debugging — configurable to 0 retention for strict accounts.
- KV / prefix cache: kept in-memory and in-region; cache eviction policies apply.
- Snapshots: hourly snapshots retained 7 days; full backups subject to your backup policy (we set 90-day overwrite by default).
- Audit logs: stored for 365 days in EU (append-only + write-once logs). We can extend retention on request.

4) Deletion proof and artifacts we can provide
- Deletion manifest (CSV) listing object paths, snapshot IDs, timestamps, and SHA256 checksums of the deleted objects.
- Audit log extracts showing erasure events with operator / automation identity and an immutable sequence number.
- Snapshot lifecycle records and storage lifecycle operations with timestamps (S3 DELETE markers, object version IDs).
- Operator affidavit + compact SCCs addendum stating transfer controls in place (see attached redwood_sccs_addendum.pdf).

Attachments we will include in the bundle: redwood_sccs_addendum.pdf, deletion_manifest_template.csv, inregion_network_diagram.png.

5) Operator access
- Human operator access for Dedicated EU-only customers is restricted by role and requires an approved JIT (just-in-time) access request. Access is logged and requires an auditable justification. For Novamed we have restricted operator access to EU-based operations staff per your contract.
- Emergency access: remote troubleshooting runs via ephemeral SSH bastions with session recording; evidence of sessions is included in the audit extracts.

Next steps:
- Jordan will prepare a sample deletion manifest and a small audit extract for the last 30 days.
- Ava will coordinate delivery and the secure upload.
- We can provide a signed operator affidavit and SCCs addendum as separate PDF attachments.

If you need any of the items signed (DocuSign) or require a prescriptive CSV layout for the deletion manifest to match your internal intake, let us know and we'll adapt.

Regards,
Naomi Feldman
Director, Privacy & Compliance, Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood_sccs_addendum.pdf (pdf, 112KB); deletion_manifest_template.csv (csv, 3KB); inregion_network_diagram.png (png, 98KB)

Subject: Renewal + capacity increase — MSA redlines and order form

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for the productive QBR last week. We're ready to move forward with an 18-month renewal and a capacity uplift for our ML inference workloads, but legal has a few redlines to the MSA and the order form that need to be agreed before we can route to procurement.

Key asks from our side:
- Term: prefer 18 months starting 2026-12-01 (we're asking for a start that aligns to our fiscal calendar).
- Capacity: increase dedicated token pool from 3M tokens/month to 5M tokens/month over the first 90 days (prorated billing ok) + optional burst block of 1M tokens for Q1.
- Billing: request net-60 payment terms and a mid-term price review clause if model infrastructure costs materially change.
- Data: clarify retention and deletion obligations for cached KV material and logs (we included tracked changes).

We would like Redwood's proposed response with redlines by EOD Monday (2026-10-12) so procurement can start the PO process.

If helpful, I can jump on a quick 30-min call next week to walk legal through comments.

Best,
Maria Gonzalez
Sr. Legal Counsel, Everline
Maria.Gonzalez@everline.com

To: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@everline.com>, contracts@everline.com

Maria — thanks for the doc and the clear asks. Appreciate the timeline.

Quick plan on our side:
1) Legal (Ben) will review the MSA redlines and provide a tracked-changes response by COB Monday.
2) Sales Ops (Laura) will review the order form uplift and confirm proration and billing cadence.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0194



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@everline.com>, contracts@everline.com
Date: Sat, 10 Oct 2026 09:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity increase — MSA redlines and order form

Folks — I reviewed Maria's redlines. High-level notes (see attached Redwood_Track_Changes_MSA_response.docx for line-level replies):

- Term: 18 months is acceptable, but we propose effective date tied to signature (with service start date optional). This avoids open-start obligations. Suggest language: "Effective Date: date of last signature; Services commence on the Service Start Date as set forth in the Order Form."
- Billing/proration: we can accommodate proration for the initial uplift; recommend explicit statement about prorated charges for partial months and a defined ramp schedule in the order form.
- Net-60: standard Redwood terms are net-30; we can offer net-45 as a concession at renewal. Net-60 would require additional credit review and Finance signoff; proposed path: grant provisional acceptance now, Finance to confirm within 48 hours.
- Indemnity & liability cap: Maria removed our mutuality language; we need to place a dollar cap tied to fees over 12 months rather than removing the cap entirely. Propose fee-based cap with carveouts for gross negligence.
- Data retention: the redline asks for immediate deletion on termination for cached KV and logs. We should accept deletion for customer-specific data but ask for narrow exception for aggregated system telemetry used for platform stability (non-identifiable). Provide precise retention window (30 days for request queue; 90 days for logs unless otherwise agreed).

Action items:
- I'll send the tracked-change response + suggested compromise language attached.
- Laura, please update the order form spreadsheet to include a 90-day ramp to the 5M token target and line items for the burst block as a separate SKU.
- Avery, after we consolidate, we should propose a 2-business-day review window for Everline to respond to our consolidated doc before we issue DocuSign. That keeps procurement timelines tight.

Ben Carter
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

title: MedBridge vendor compliance questionnaire — clarifying scope and evidence
preview:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@medbridge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurements@medbridge.com
Date: Fri, 9 Oct 2026 15:24:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor security questionnaire — MedBridge kickoff

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo earlier today — good conversation. Procurement and security asked me to send the attached questionnaire (CAIQ-derived PDF) and a short scoping spreadsheet so your team can map owners and expected evidence. We have two asks up front:

1) Confirm whether Redwood has a current SOC 2 Type II report and date (or a timeline if in progress).
2) Clarify hosting regions and whether data for MedBridge can be pinned to an EU region for EU patient data.

I've attached: MedBridge_Security_Questionnaire_CAIQ.pdf and a Scoping table (MedBridge_Scoping_Table.xlsx) — the procurement team would like an initial response within 7 business days to keep our rollout timeline.

Can you point the security team to a primary contact for evidence collection and whether a short walk-through call is helpful?

Thanks,
Elena Ruiz
Director, Procurement & Vendor Risk
MedBridge Health
[attachment: MedBridge_Security_Questionnaire_CAIQ.pdf (PDF)]
[attachment: MedBridge_Scoping_Table.xlsx (Excel)]

---
Forwarded message included below when recipients reply to specific sections; please preserve original numbering when responding.

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@medbridge.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 9 Oct 2026 16:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Vendor security questionnaire — MedBridge kickoff

Elena — thanks for the materials and for the clear timeline. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side:

- SOC 2: Redwood maintains a SOC 2 Type II report; our most recent audit period ended 2026-03-31. We can share the report under an NDA (or via your secure upload portal if you prefer).
- Hosting/Residency: Redwood supports region pinning. For EU residency we offer EU regions for both hosting and log storage on Private/Dedicated plans; Hosted API customers can opt into EU-only data residency — we need to confirm which product configuration MedBridge plans to use (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private).

Clarifying questions (to help us map evidence):
1) Is the request scoped to production traffic only, or do you also need information for non-prod/test systems and backups?
2) For HIPAA/GDPR applicability: is MedBridge asking for HIPAA controls only for PHI flows, or do you need an attestation for handling PHI in integrations?
3) On encryption and key management: do you require KMS/HSM evidence with customer-managed keys (CMKs) or will provider-managed keys suffice for initial review?

Proposed actions:
- Kimberly will own the evidence bundle and fill the scoping table with owner assignments (expected Monday).
- I'll provide an initial SOC 2 access path and a short summary doc for the items you flagged by EOD Monday (2026-10-12).
- Recommend a 30-minute follow-up call with your security team next week to walk the CAIQ mapping (we can do Thurs 10/15 or Fri 10/16; open to other times).

If that works, Kimberly, can you take point on the scoping table and add internal owners? Jordan — please slot time to assemble the SOC2 artifact and the minimal evidence set for encryption/KMS.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
[attachment: redwood-soc2-redacted-README.txt (TXT) — contains sharing instructions]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@medbridge.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Vendor security questionnaire — MedBridge kickoff

Elena / Ben / Jordan — I've started filling the scoping table; attaching my draft mapping as inline answers in the spreadsheet (see attached rows). A few clarifications from the evidence side:

- Scope: We recommend MedBridge limit the initial formal evidence to production systems and their backups. Non-prod test environments have different controls and often require separate attestations — happy to include a statement but it will increase the delivery time.
- SOC 2 sharing: We can share a redacted SOC 2 report via a secure link and provide the auditor contact under NDA. If MedBridge prefers an eSign NDA, send the DocuSign link and I'll route.
- EU residency: For Hosted API customers we can enable EU log residency but full EU-hosted inference requires either Dedicated or Private deployment; we can propose a migration plan if you plan to keep traffic pinned to EU.
- KMS: Provider-managed keys are acceptable for the questionnaire answers initially; if you need CMKs (customer-managed keys) we will surface timing and platform requirements — that is typically an onboarding project.

Attachments: MedBridge_Scoping_Table_Fill.xlsx (partial) — includes owner column and estimated ETA for evidence.

Next steps I suggest:
- Elena, confirm whether initial scope should include backups and non-prod.
- If yes, we will add an extra 5 business days to the ETA. If no, we will target your 7-business-day window.
- Confirm preference for SOC 2 transfer (secure link vs DocuSign NDA).

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Security & Compliance Program Manager
Redwood Inference
[attachment: MedBridge_Scoping_Table_Fill.xlsx (Excel)]

- We need confirmation of which PO header fields Redwood requires (we have separate ERP mappings that require exact header names).

- We prefer monthly split invoices (one invoice per month per billing entity) rather than consolidated quarterly invoices.

Subject: Re: PO fields, net60 request and monthly split invoices

Hi Marta — great to hear and thanks for sharing the PO template. Short answers up front, then a suggested path: 

1) PO header fields: we require the following exact fields for ERP matching: PO Number, Bill-To Entity (legal entity name), Bill-To Address, Tax ID / EIN, Billing Contact (email), Invoice Frequency, Currency, and Redwood Vendor Code. If Silverline wants invoice line-level SKU mapping include 'SKU' and 'Line Description'. I'll attach a Redwood invoice template (redwood-invoice-template.xlsx) so you can see the export format our AR team consumes.

2) Net terms: Our standard for new enterprise netting is net45. We can make an exception to net60, subject to a quick credit review and a D&B/References check. If procurement can accept a one-cycle net60 (first three invoices) and then net45 thereafter, that is something Finance will allow to mitigate risk. Alternatively, we can offer net60 on the condition of a 5% prepayment to offset working capital — happy to discuss.

3) Monthly split invoices: Yes, we can issue one invoice per month per billing entity. We just need the PO to indicate the invoice frequency and the PO should reference the Billing Entity code that matches your ERP. We cannot accept ad-hoc line-item remapping after invoices are issued (it creates reconciliation overhead).

4) Discount & packaging: Discount eligibility is tied to committed ARR, billing cadence, and whether invoices are issued net30/net45 vs net60. For a 12-month commitment at $250k ARR with monthly invoicing, our standard commercial can include a 5% committed-volume discount. Higher discounts (7-10%) are possible if Silverline agrees to quarterly advance billing or a partial upfront prepayment schedule.

Proposed next steps (mutual action plan):
- You confirm the exact Bill-To Entity name and PO header sample (I can validate against our template).
- We run a quick credit check and Finance gives conditional sign-off for net60 for the first three billing cycles.
- We issue a contract amendment reflecting the payment terms and the 5% volume discount; both sides sign via DocuSign.
- Procurement issues PO referencing Redwood Vendor Code: RW-2311 and the Bill-To Entity code.

I'm attaching the redwood-invoice-template.xlsx and will loop in Finance internally to expedite the netterms conversation.

Subject: Marketplace MAP: staging acceptance, procurement milestones & billing handoff for NovaPulse

Hi Diane / Alex —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. As promised, attached is a concise MAP for the GCP private-offer path we discussed. The goal here is to align milestones across Engineering, Procurement, and Finance so your deployment and invoice flow are predictable. Key items in this version:

- Staged acceptance gates (smoke test → production validation → SLA handoff) with owner and target dates
- Procurement checklist for your SOW signoff and PO to the marketplace private-offer
- Billing handoff: recommended invoice cadence and account alias to avoid double-billing in GCP

Action items for this week:
1) Procurement: confirm required legal language for marketplace POs (payment term / remittance).
2) Engineering: confirm acceptance checklist items and a target 7-day validation window post-deploy.
3) Finance: confirm preferred invoice recipient and GL code for mapping.

I attached: NovaPulse_MAP_v3.pdf (MAP), NovaPulse_SOW_lineitems.xlsx (SOW line items), and a short invoice guidance text file.

If that looks good I can push the DocuSign packet for the SOW and coordinate an acceptance window starting Oct 19.

Hi Lauren —

Appreciate the clear MAP and the attachments. A couple of quick clarifying questions from Procurement before we confirm dates:

1) For the marketplace private-offer, can Redwood accept a PO referencing the private-offer SKU and our GCP billing alias? Our accounting system requires the invoice to list the marketplace offer id.

3) On acceptance: can you confirm a rollback plan and who owns the last-mile validation failures (NovaPulse vs Redwood) during the 7-day validation window?

Subject: Re: Marketplace MAP: staging acceptance, procurement milestones & billing handoff for NovaPulse

Quick answers for Procurement items — tagging Legal/Finance in-thread for confirmation but want to keep the momentum:

1) Marketplace invoice: Yes, we can map the private-offer SKU to your GCP billing alias. We'll include the marketplace offer id and your alias on the invoice. Operational note: for a GCP private-offer the marketplace issues the invoice, but Redwood will provide the required billing metadata to ensure your AR system matches it.

2) Payment terms: We can accept Net 45 on the invoice routed through marketplace BUT we need the signed SOW to reflect the acceptance gates and the billing recipient. If Finance approves (cc'd), we can confirm in writing.

3) Acceptance ownership: Redwood will own service-level validation and remediation for any infra/runtime-related failures during the 7-day validation. NovaPulse will own any customer-data-specific integration regressions (mapping/data schema errors). We'll include a short rollback play in the acceptance appendix.

Next steps I recommend: Legal & Finance confirm Net 45 ok (cc'd), Diana/Procurement confirm PO alias and remit details, and we schedule a short 30-min close call Thursday to freeze dates.

Thanks —
