title: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options
preview:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Jenna Ortiz <jenna@slate.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options

Hi Jenna,

Thanks again for the time last week. I pulled together two straightforward options for Slate's upcoming renewal based on the utilization profile for Q3 (attached):

Current baseline: 40 reserved GPUs (A100 equiv), 24/7. Observed patterns: sustained 60-70% utilization on weekdays with frequent 90%+ mini-spikes during model retrain windows and nightly batch jobs. Tail latency increases when short (1–2 hour) peak waves occur.

Option A — Conservative uplift + burst: +9 reserved GPUs (49 total) + 16-GPU burst pool for peak windows. This keeps monthly committed spend close to today while covering observed peaks with burst capacity.

Option B — Phased reserve step-up: +12 reserved GPUs immediately (52 total), 3-month review to consider moving 6 of those into a scheduled burst pool if average utilization normalizes. This is the lower operational risk path for latency-sensitive workloads.

Short ask: can you confirm which appetite Slate has for committed spend vs. relying on burst credits? If you prefer, we can run a 6‑week controlled pilot for Option A (monitoring SLOs and unit economics) before committing to Option B.

Attached: slate_q3_utilization.csv, draft_amendment_slate_v2.pdf (proposal + cost delta table).

If it helps, I can set up a 30-minute sync with our solutions + finance folks to walk through the cost math and SLO guarantees.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
kimberly@redwood.com
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: slate_q3_utilization.csv (type: text/csv)
Attachment: draft_amendment_slate_v2.pdf (type: application/pdf)

From: Jenna Ortiz <jenna@slate.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options

Kimberly,

Appreciate the breakdown and the files. A few quick notes from our side:

- Procurement timeline: our FY27 budget window opens Oct 20; we need a signed SOW by Oct 25 to hit the Q4 spend plan.
- Cost sensitivity: we can stretch committed spend a bit but prefer not to double the baseline without clear ROI.
- Risk tolerance: latency-sensitive prod routes can't rely solely on on-demand bursting; predictable headroom preferred.

Given that, I'm leaning hybrid — something close to Option A but with a smaller immediate commit uplift and a well-defined review cadence. Before we sign anything, procurement will need the delta numbers broken down monthly and a simple SLA addendum for latency behavior during peak windows.

Can Ben or someone on your side run a 90‑day projected cost / SLO impact for a +9 reserved GPU scenario with a 16-GPU burst pool? Also please include how we would handle short retrain spikes (2–3 hours) and any automatic failovers if the burst pool is saturated.

Looping in our procurement alias (procurement@slate.ai) for visibility.

Thanks,
Jenna Ortiz
Head of Platform, Slate AI
jenna@slate.ai

On Mon, Oct 5, 2026 at 08:12 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original message]


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Jenna Ortiz <jenna@slate.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 10:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options

Hey Jenna — quick cost + ops sketch attached (also dropped a short calc in the Drive link). Key points: 

- Measured tail: 95th-percentile token latency climbs ~40% when aggregate utilization >80% because of queuing in the serving pool. That explains the customer-reported latency blips.
- For immediate protection we recommend +10 reserved GPUs (49 -> 50 assuming rounding) paired with an auto-burst window sized to 16 GPUs that kicks in when the 5-min rolling utilization exceeds 72%. That gives ~2.5x more thermal headroom for 1–3 hour spikes.
- Cost impact: our model shows ~+11% monthly committed spend vs current for +10 GPUs, with a blended spend (committed + burst) ~+18% for the blended months we assume spikes. If you want to cap budget delta, we can propose a staged billing (first 3 months at blended rate, then reprice based on realized hours).
- Operational guardrails: automatic fallback to smaller model instances when burst is saturated, plus prioritized routing for your prod routes. We also recommend a 6-week pilot on a subset of prod traffic to verify tail behavior before full cutover.

I'll attach the calculator and a one-pager SLO amendment for sign-off.

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

--
Attachment: slate_cost_calc_q3_v1.xlsx (type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

From: Jenna Ortiz <jenna@slate.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@slate.ai>
Date: Fri, 08 Oct 2026 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options

Kimberly/Ben,

Thanks — this is helpful. Procurement has reviewed the initial numbers and is willing to proceed on a hybrid path:

- Agreement: add +9 reserved GPUs to the existing commit (total 49).
- Burst: enable a 16‑GPU burst pool for up to 120 hours/month.
- Pilot & review: 6‑week pilot starting on activation, with a retro review to re-evaluate whether 3 of the reserved GPUs can be reclassified into burst capacity.
- SLA addendum: procure will require the latency SLO addendum for prod routes (please attach final language to the SOW).

Next steps from our side: procurement needs an updated SOW and a DocuSign link by Oct 20 to route internally. Please include the monthly delta table and a contact for technical escalation.

Procurement contact: Maya Lin <maya@slate.ai> (cc’d).

If you can get the SOW + DocuSign before EOD next Tuesday (Oct 12), we’ll prioritize the internal approval.

Best,
Jenna Ortiz
Head of Platform, Slate AI

Can Ben or someone on your side run a 90‑day projected cost / SLO impact for a +9 reserved GPU scenario with a 16-GPU burst pool? Also please include how we would handle short retrain spikes (2–3 hours) and any automatic failovers if the burst pool is saturated.

Looping in our procurement alias (procurement@slate.ai) for visibility.

Thanks,
Jenna Ortiz
Head of Platform, Slate AI
jenna@slate.ai

- For immediate protection we recommend +10 reserved GPUs (49 -> 50 assuming rounding) paired with an auto-burst window sized to 16 GPUs that kicks in when the 5-min rolling utilization exceeds 72%. That gives ~2.5x more thermal headroom for 1–3 hour spikes.
- Cost impact: our model shows ~+11% monthly committed spend vs current for +10 GPUs, with a blended spend (committed + burst) ~+18% for the blended months we assume spikes. If you want to cap budget delta, we can propose a staged billing (first 3 months at blended rate, then reprice based on realized hours).
- Operational guardrails: automatic fallback to smaller model instances when burst is saturated, plus prioritized routing for your prod routes. We also recommend a 6-week pilot on a subset of prod traffic to verify tail behavior before full cutover.

I'll attach the calculator and a one-pager SLO amendment for sign-off.

Ben Carter

Sarah —

Thanks again for taking a look at the draft privacy annex. I’ve attached our initial proposed edits (privacy_annex_redline_v1.docx) and a short note about hosting options we referenced in the order form. A few specific asks to help speed legal review:

- Can you confirm whether Acme prefers the EU SCCs (2021) or the modular Addendum approach for this engagement? We can accommodate either but need to align on the exact clause set for the order form.
- We redlined the standard processor/processor role table — please confirm whether you want a controller-to-controller carveout for model fine-tuning training data (we flagged Clause 6.3).
- On KMS/hosting: Acme asked about customer-managed keys in a VPC private deployment. I’ve included a one-page dataflow graphic (redwood_kms_dataflow.png). If you need a short architecture note for your security team I can pull one together.

Would you be available for a 30-minute sync the week of Oct 12 to walk the redlines and any mandatory evidence requests? If helpful, Laura (legal ops) and Olga (privacy engineering) are on cc and will join the call.

Attachments: privacy_annex_redline_v1.docx, redwood_kms_dataflow.png

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0132

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Legal Team <legal@acmehealth.com>
Date: Wed, 7 Oct 2026 15:24:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Privacy annex: initial redline and hosting/transfer questions

Ben —

Thanks for the draft and the KMS diagram. I’ve attached ACME_DPA_comments.pdf with inline comments and a short SCCs_note.docx where our counsel has proposed preferred language. High level: we generally prefer the modular addendum + 2021 SCCs hybrid approach for commercial flexibility, but there are three items that will be gating for procurement:

1) Liability caps tied to processing (we proposed an explicit carveout in Section 10).
2) Clarification of subprocessor notification timing — we proposed a 10-day notice for changes that materially affect EU-resident data.
3) KMS and key access model — procurement will require a short bullet list describing who holds keys, what logs are available, and whether keys are exportable.

Attached: ACME_DPA_comments.pdf (inline redlines), SCCs_note.docx (preferred clause language). I’ve also cc’d our legal mailbox for record.

We can do Oct 12 at 10:00 UK time for a 30-minute call — that would be 02:00 PT though, so Laura or Olga might want to propose another slot if earlier works. If you can send a short draft response on the three gating items above before the call, that will help our counsel triage the review.

title: Post-call: StratumAI throughput & latency diagnostic and next steps
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Mehta <sanjay@stratumai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: StratumAI throughput & latency diagnostic and next steps

Hi Sanjay — thanks again for hopping on earlier. Quick written recap and concrete next steps so we’re aligned. I’ve attached the sample payload set you mentioned (stratum_payload_samples.json) and the QPS snapshot CSV.

Summary of what we heard today
- Primary pain: interactive latency spikes during peak ingress (not steady-state). Customers see long tails at the 95th/99th percentiles even when p50 looks OK.
- Secondary pain: throughput collapse when a meaningful fraction of requests are long (>=1024 tokens) and when KV-cache warmup is poor.
- Constraint: data residency for logs and audit trails (SOC2 / enterprise). You confirmed deployment needs to be in us-west2 with VPC peering.

Goals you called out
- Interactive UX: target p95 <= 350 ms for short-to-medium prompts (<=256 tokens).
- Throughput: sustained 40–60 concurrent QPS for typical bursts, with ability to scale to 120 QPS short-term for campaign spikes.
- Quality: retain current model output fidelity; avoid regressions vs your existing Stratum-Lite model.

Initial topology proposal (high-level)
- Dedicated pool (warm path) sized for steady traffic + small hosted fallback pool for overflow.
- Prefix/KV cache for repeated context to cut down transformer work on common prompts. 60s–300s TTL depending on prompt churn.
- Model variants: run primary (Stratum-Lite / equivalent ~13B) and a quantized fallback for cost-sensitive routes.
- Autoscaling: policy by tail-latency and queue length; controlled burst provision (reserve hot slots).

What we need from you to size the POC and benchmark accurately
1) Representative request samples (attached sample set is our placeholder; please confirm or replace).
2) Historical p50/p95/p99 histograms and a short 1-hour sample trace around a peak spike (timestamps + request ids).
3) Expected token-length distribution (percentiles or buckets).
4) Any blacklists/filters for telemetry we should exclude from shared logs for privacy/compliance.

Proposed next steps
- You: send the 1-hour peak trace + CSV of request shape by EOD Wed 10/7.
- Redwood: produce an initial benchmark plan and a recommended Dedicated pool sizing by Fri 10/9.
- Schedule a 60m POC kickoff (we suggested next Tue/Wed); we’ll run three benchmark passes (baseline, quantized, caching-on).

Attachments: stratum_payload_samples.json, stratum-qps-snapshot.csv

Thanks — I’ll follow up once we get your traces and can lock the POC window.

—
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Redwood Inference



From: Sanjay Mehta <sanjay@stratumai.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: eng+ops@stratumai.com
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call: StratumAI throughput & latency diagnostic and next steps

Thanks Ava — great write-up. Quick confirmations and I’ve attached the two files you requested (stratum_payload_samples.json and stratum-qps-snapshot.csv).

Confirmations / clarifications:
- Our internal SLA goal for public-facing chat is 99th < 600 ms, but product team prioritizes p95 <= 300 ms for the main interactive route.
- Model: we’re currently on Stratum-Lite (proprietary, ~13B equivalence). Average payload today is ~480 tokens, median ~210; long tail for some agents hits >1500 tokens.
- Peak behavior: we see short-lived spikes when campaigns launch — typical ramp is 2–5 minutes, then plateaus for 10–30 min.
- Data: will share 1-hour peak trace with request ids by EOD Wed (10/7). There are a couple fields redacted for PII but should be sufficient.

Proposed POC timing: next Wed 10/14 or Thu 10/15. We can give a 2-hour window for live traffic replay + 1 hour for debrief. Prefer Wed 10/14 10:00–12:00 PT.

I’ll loop in our infra lead (eng+ops) to coordinate VPC peering details and credentials for the benchmark harness.

—
Sanjay Mehta
VP Product, StratumAI
sanjay@stratumai.com

On Mon, Oct 5, 2026 at 03:12 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Sanjay — thanks again for hopping on earlier. Quick written recap and concrete next steps so we’re aligned. I’ve attached the sample payload set you mentioned (stratum_payload_samples.json) and the QPS snapshot CSV.
>
> [original message trimmed for brevity]



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Mehta <sanjay@stratumai.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call: StratumAI throughput & latency diagnostic and next steps

Thanks — a few technical clarifications so we size the Dedicated pool correctly and craft the benchmark plan. I’ll be running the POC bench with our performance team and want to be precise on the inputs below.

1) Traffic shape and baseline numbers (please confirm):
   - Median token length: 210 (Sanjay said median ~210)
   - Mean token length: 480 (long tail drives this up)
   - Target sustained concurrency for benchmark: 50 QPS, with a 120 QPS short burst (2–5 minutes)

2) Target latency SLOs to optimize for (prioritize in order):
   - p95 <= 350 ms for <=256-token requests
   - p99 <= 600 ms overall
   - cold start target for scaled up nodes: < 2s for first requests (we’ll validate via warmup strategy)

3) Suggested bench passes (we’ll run each 3x):
   A) Baseline: current Stratum-Lite model on default settings (no cache, no quantization)
   B) Quantized path: 4-bit quant (if compatible) + kernel-level optimizations (fused attention kernels)
   C) Cache-enabled: prefix/KV cache with 60s TTL and automatic eviction for >5k unique keys

4) Infrastructure sizing estimate (initial proposal for POC):
   - For 50 QPS @ median 210 tokens: 2 x A10G-equivalent dedicated nodes (sized for low-latency small-batch execution).
   - To support 120 QPS burst without >p95 target slippage: add burst reserve of 4 warm slots (hot processes) and enable managed fallback to hosted pool for overflow.

5) Bench metrics we need you to provide in the trace:
   - Request id, arrival timestamp, completion timestamp, token_count, model_variant, success/error code

Action items
- Sanjay: confirm token distribution file + share the 1-hour peak trace (we need request ids in CSV).
- Stratum infra: confirm if the model binary supports 4-bit quantization; if not, tell us best compatible quant profile.
- Redwood: we’ll return a concrete node count + bench script and a pre-populated JMeter/Locust config. Link to our bench harness: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1examplebenchmark

Notes on KV cache and correctness
- Cache gives the biggest win on repeated prefixes (common prompts). For correctness-sensitive intents we’ll implement route-level opt-in so we only use cached responses for idempotent/low-risk flows.

If you can confirm the token distribution CSV and the quant support by EOD Wed (10/7), we’ll lock the POC for Wed 10/14 10:00 PT.

—
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

Subject: Re: Post-call: StratumAI throughput & latency diagnostic and next steps

Appended a short initial benchmark plan and the minimal config we’ll use for the POC (I attached initial-benchmark-plan.md to the thread in Gmail). Two quick pragmatic suggestions before the run:

1) Narrow the initial test set to 500 representative requests (mix short/medium/long per your buckets). That is enough to reproduce tail behavior in the lab and keeps data redaction easier.

2) For the first pass, let’s disable any complex post-processing you may run client-side (that can mask server latency). Send us a 500-row CSV with request_id, prompt, token_count, and whether the request is interactive vs batch.

Benchmark config highlights (what we’ll report back):
- Metrics: p50/p95/p99 latency, tokens/sec, GPU utilization, memory pressure, failed requests rate, bytes in/out
- Model variants: Stratum-Lite (baseline), Stratum-Lite-quant4 (if avail), Redwood-fallback-quant (ours)
- Cache config: 60s TTL, 4KB max key size, LRU eviction

Sanjay — can you confirm the 500-request set and the availability of the quant profile by EOD Wed (10/7)? If yes, we’ll lock Wed 10/14 10:00 PT for the POC.

—
Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

title: Week of 2026-10-04: Exec staff update — QBR packaging & a Greenleaf escalation
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Exec Leadership Distro <leadership@redwood.com>; Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>; Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>; Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 08:13:00 -0700
Subject: Week of 2026-10-04: Exec staff update — QBR packaging & a Greenleaf escalation

Good morning all,

Quick weekly exec staff note ahead of QBR pack grooming this afternoon. I kept it tight — wins / risks / asks. Attachment: 2026Q3_exec_brief.pdf (slide deck stub).

Wins:
- Hosted API latency improvements: median p50 reduced from 68ms to 55ms after the LRU cache fix; will show microbench on slide 4.
- Dedicated contract signed with NovaHealth (pilot starts 10/20) — +$120k ARR projected in FY27.
- Model onboarding pipeline: two new quantized variants now in the catalog (lower cost, similar perf).

Risks / open items:
- Greenleaf Analytics (DEAL-8421) reported a tail-latency regression on 4k context during peak — legal and SRE are looping, but this may surface in QBR if not resolved. See Jordan's forwarded note below.
- QBR pack: our slide on unit economics needs updated token-cost delta after the last batch optimizer change (ask to Finance & Runtime).
- Canaries for private deployments are showing a slightly higher error rate (0.6% -> 1.1%) for one region; we should decide whether to call this OUTAGE or degradation for the pack.

Asks (short):
1) Evelyn — can you own the CS/Canary action and provide a short summary (1 slide) with mitigation plan by EOD?
2) Laura — need ARR impact table and the headcount ask language for the exec pack by 15:00 PT.
3) Jordan — please share the Greenleaf escalation thread and any proposed customer-facing remediation.

I'll be in the QBR grooming at 15:30; attaching the working deck for comments.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: 2026Q3_exec_brief.pdf

Looping everyone in on the customer escalation. I flagged this P1 to SRE and Infra; CS is coordinating an interim config rollback that should reduce tail latencies within the hour. Legal is already on the redlines thread.

We've been seeing large latency spikes in the 4k-context endpoints between 11:30-12:30 ET across multiple accounts. This started after the optimizer push yesterday. title: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>, Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Hi Elena / Tom,

Quick note as we approach your upcoming renewal window (Nov 1): thanks for the usage update last quarter — looks like Novaforge is trending ~45% above your current monthly commit during peak hours. To avoid throttle-induced latency and to keep unit economics predictable, I want to propose a short-term commit uplift for Q4 with a seasonal burst pocket for Black Friday/Cyber Monday traffic.

High level proposal (draft):
- Monthly committed tokens: increase from 90M -> 140M (hosted API)
- Tier move: Standard -> Premium (reduced per-token price at scale)
- Burst pocket: 25M tokens/month (one-time monthly allowance for peaks, bumped to consume before overage)
- Soft throttle buffer: allow 10% headroom for sudden spikes; billing applies only after burst pocket is consumed

Attached: draft mutual action plan and a short SOW amendment (stubs attached). If this looks reasonable I can circulate a redline-ready SOW and a DocuSign link.

Can we sync Tuesday for a 30-min ops + finance call? Kimberly will join for invoicing cadence.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
(415) 555-0187

Attachments: Novaforge_MAPlan_v2.pdf (PDF, 2 pages), Amendment_SOW_Novaforge.docx (DOCX, placeholder)

From: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Ben — thanks for the heads up and for drafting this. A few quick questions before we commit:

1) Pricing sensitivity: can you share the per-token price at the 90M, 140M, and 200M commit bands? We want to model cost vs expected traffic and see where the breakpoint is.
2) Burst behavior: does the burst pocket bypass rate limits or is it consumed within the same rate window? If our peak is very short (seconds-long spikes) we need to ensure the rate cap won’t throttle important user flows.
3) Term: would you be open to a 9-month ramp (Nov–Jul) instead of annual if the numbers are preferable?

Also looping Tom — he heads infra and will want a quick read on rate limit guarantees.

—Elena
Elena Morales
VP Product, Novaforge
elena@novaforge.ai

On Thu, Oct 1, 2026 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick note as we approach your upcoming renewal window (Nov 1): thanks for the usage update...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>, Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 03 Oct 2026 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Elena — great questions. I've pulled in the numbers and a short pricing matrix below. Marcus added a technical note in the next message about rate caps and burst semantics; please see that for the infra detail.

Pricing (hosted API) — illustrative net pricing after committed discount:
- 90M (current): $0.00028 / token
- 140M (proposal): $0.000225 / token
- 200M (stretch): $0.000195 / token

Burst pocket behavior:
- Burst pocket is a monthly token allowance billed at the committed rate but consumed prior to overage. It does not change per-second rate limits (QPS) on a single connection. For short spikes we recommend a combination of a small dedicated capacity reservation (if latency is critical) + burst pocket usage to avoid billing surprises.

Term flexibility: we can do a 9-month ramp with pro-rated commit months, but pricing is more favorable on the 12-month annualized commit. I can show the blended effective rate for both options on the call.

Next steps I propose:
1) Technical confirm with Marcus on rate characteristics (done below).
2) Joint call 30m Mon — ops + finance — Kimberly to own invoicing cadence; Tom / Marcus for infra.
3) I’ll circulate a redline-friendly SOW and DocuSign link once we align on tier and term.

If that works I’ll lock a provisional notch in our capacity plan for Nov.

Ben



From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>, Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 04 Oct 2026 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Tom / Elena — quick technical note from infra/SE side so you have clarity before the call.

- Rate limits: hosted API enforces per-key QPS limits and a per-account aggregated concurrency window. Burst pockets affect token accounting, not instantaneous QPS caps. If your user flows need headroom across short spikes (sub-minute), the QPS limiter is the constraint.
- Recommendation: for Black Friday traffic spikes we typically recommend a small Dedicated reservation (single ranked pool) sized to cover peak QPS; that ensures per-request latency stays stable. The billing model would be: committed hosted tokens + small dedicated reservation (monthly fee) + burst pocket for overflow consumption.
- Optimizations: enable prefix KV-cache for repeated prompts in ad-serving flow and turn on auto-batching for small prompt sizes; both reduce per-token compute and latency. Quantization (4-bit) can also reduce cost if you onboard a compatible model variant.

Happy to scope dedicated sizing if you share peak QPS/avg token length.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

title: Post-call: Procurement & marketplace path (Azure/AWS options) - Catalyze Health
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Lee <sam.lee@catalyzehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-04T14:10:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call: Procurement & marketplace path (Azure/AWS options) - Catalyze Health

Hi Sam —

Great to meet earlier today. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we’re aligned on procurement and commercial path: 

Summary of call
- Use case: AI-assisted care-coordination triage (prototype today, pilot Q1)
- Expected Q1 pilot spend: small but will scale if results are positive
- Primary infra preference: Azure region but open to AWS Marketplace if it simplifies purchasing

Procurement questions we need to confirm
1) Purchase vehicle: PO (Net 30) vs. card? You mentioned finance prefers PO — can you confirm?
2) Marketplace interest: Is AWS Marketplace (Private Offer) acceptable, or do you need direct vendor invoicing?
3) Required docs: Any standard onboarding forms (W-9, vendor questionnaire, SOC 2/attestation)?
4) Timeline: target to have a PO in place by mid-November?

Proposed next steps
- I’m attaching a brief mutual action plan (MUTUAL_ACTION_PLAN.xlsx) with milestones (tech eval, security review, PO issuance).
- If PO is the path, can you introduce your procurement contact and preferred PO template?
- If marketplace is preferred, confirm which marketplace (AWS/GCP/Azure) and whether a Private Offer is required.

I’ll follow up with our sales ops and legal to get the minimal vendor packet ready once I have the procurement contact.

Thanks — looking forward to lining this up.

Best,
Ben Carter | Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: MUTUAL_ACTION_PLAN.xlsx (excel, 42KB)

Date: 2026-10-04T16:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: Procurement & marketplace path (Azure/AWS options) - Catalyze Health

Ben —

Thanks for the recap and the clear MAP. Quick answers below: 

> On 10/4/26 2:10 PM, Ben Carter wrote:
> > Procurement questions we need to confirm

1) Purchase vehicle: We do PO (Net 30) for anything > $5k. For pilots we can still do PO.
2) Marketplace: We prefer AWS Marketplace if it means faster procurement — we have a procurement carve-out for Marketplace purchases. That said, our infra team prefers Azure; we'd accept the model running in Azure if licensing/invoicing is handled via AWS Marketplace Private Offer (we know it's weird).
3) Required docs: Procurement needs vendor W-9, our security team wants SOC 2 Type II (or an equivalent attestation), and the onboarding questionnaire you mentioned.
4) Timeline: We aim to cut PO within ~3 weeks once legal/vendor packet is in. Pilot kickoff target: mid-November (tight but possible).

I’m looping in procurement now: procurement@catalyzehealth.com — they’ll coordinate templates and PO routing. Please include any vendor intake form and a short SOW they can attach to the PO.

Sam Lee
Head of Product
Catalyze Health
sam.lee@catalyzehealth.com

- I’ll also attach a short one-page SOW and a sample invoice layout so your PO lines match.

To procurement: What is your preferred PO template (PDF or web form)? Any vendor codes or tax info you need ahead of the packet? If you can share the PO routing (email + AP contact) that helps avoid delays.

Appreciate it — I’ll stand by for the procurement contact and will escalate the Marketplace option if that will speed the PO.

title: Post-demo: AWS Marketplace private offer — unit economics and next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Chen <lisa@acmehealth.com>, Sophie Reed <sophie.reed@aws-partners.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Connor Obrien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-05 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: AWS Marketplace private offer — unit economics and next steps

Hi Lisa / Sophie — great demo this morning, thanks for the time. Quick recap and the asks we agreed on: 

1) Volume & Budget (from Acme): Lisa mentioned an initial footprint of ~35M tokens/month with a possible ramp to 60M in Q2. Budget guidance is roughly $25k–$40k/month for inference consumption. Please flag if that’s off.

2) Target unit economics: Acme is targeting a landed price in the ~$800–$1,100 per 1M tokens range to make the POC economics work for their care-coordination app. 

3) Marketplace path: We discussed using an AWS Marketplace private offer so Acme can pay via their existing procurement flow. Sophie — you flagged that the private offer needs a SKU/unit mapping that will appear on the AWS invoice; we need to confirm the gross vs net mapping and any marketplace fee passthroughs.

4) Next steps we agreed:
- Ben (me) to send a first-draft unit-price mapping and a simple per-month cost table (attached: acme-private-offer-econ-draft.xlsx).
- Sophie to confirm the required line-item format for a private offer (SKU name, unit multiplier, any marketplace fees).
- Acme procurement to confirm PO timing and whether a 12-month commitment is plausible.

Attached is a slide + spreadsheet that maps a few unit-price scenarios (rows: 800, 950, 1,100 $/1M tokens) across the expected token curve (35M -> 60M). I shaded the rows that meet Acme’s current budget band.

Can you both confirm the SKU naming constraints and whether the marketplace fee will be visible as a separate line item or if it must be grossed into the unit price? Also please confirm the token volume assumptions above so we can lock a first private-offer draft.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0198

Attachments: acme-private-offer-econ-draft.xlsx (spreadsheet), redwood-marketplace-ppu-guidance.pdf (one-pager)

From: Lisa Chen <lisa@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@acmehealth.com
Date: 2026-10-05 17:01:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: AWS Marketplace private offer — unit economics and next steps

Thanks Ben — this matches our internal readout. A few clarifications from Acme procurement: 

- Monthly volume: we expect 30–40M tokens average first 3 months, with a potential jump to ~55–65M if the pilot metrics hit the retention thresholds.
- Budget: leadership signed off on a ceiling of $40k/month for the pilot phase; they prefer staying closer to $25–30k/month to keep ops margin intact.
- PO cadence: procurement would prefer a 12-month term if it helps lower the unit price, otherwise they will do quarterly renewals.

Action for Redwood: please include a 12-month committed rate and a non-committed (month-to-month) uplift in the mapping so we can show both options to finance. Also we need line items to map to AWS invoice for internal chargeback.

Lisa
Lisa Chen
Head of Product, Acme Health
lisa@acmehealth.com

On Oct 5, 2026, at 15:12, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)

From: Sophie Reed <sophie.reed@aws-partners.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Lisa Chen <lisa@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-05 20:33:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: AWS Marketplace private offer — unit economics and next steps

Ben / Lisa —
Thanks for the clear recap. A couple of specifics from the AWS Marketplace side that will impact how you (Redwood) model the PPU: 

- SKU naming: Private offers must include a SKU string and a unit definition. For meter-based billing we usually ask partners to provide a SKU like redwood_token_1M and a unit multiplier (1 = per 1M tokens). That SKU will appear on the customer invoice.
- Marketplace fees: AWS applies a marketplace fee (varies by program). In most private offers the fee is taken out after invoicing (AWS pays the partner net), so partners typically present gross list price and note the net payable after marketplace fee. If Acme needs a single-line charge, that can be done, but total list price needs to be higher to account for the fee.
- PO formatting: procurement will want the SKU string visible so they can map to internal GL codes. AGGREGATION: if Redwood wants to offer a single blended unit (to hide the fee), that’s possible but procurement may ask for the breakdown for audit.

I attached an AWS private-offer example template. If you can send a spreadsheet with: (a) desired unit price per 1M tokens (gross), (b) whether this is committed or on-demand, (c) expected months at each tier, we can confirm if the SKU mapping meets invoicing needs.

Best,
Sophie Reed
Marketplace Partner Lead, AWS
sophie.reed@aws-partners.com

Attachments: aws-private-offer-template.xlsx

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Reed <sophie.reed@aws-partners.com>, Lisa Chen <lisa@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Connor Obrien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-06 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: AWS Marketplace private offer — unit economics and next steps

Thanks Sophie — very helpful. I've updated the draft spreadsheet (attached) to include two rows for each scenario: gross list price and net-to-partner after a 12% marketplace fee (example). Highlights: 

- Committed 12-month pricing examples (per 1M tokens): $800 (gross) -> $704 (net), $950 -> $836, $1,100 -> $968.
- Month-to-month (no commit): add a 20% uplift on gross in the sheet.
- Example cost at 35M tokens/mo: at $950 gross = ~$33,250/mo gross; net to Redwood after fee ~ $29,260/mo.

Karthik — can you please confirm: (a) whether Redwood can model the private-offer as a single SKU (redwood_token_1M) with the marketplace fee grossed, or (b) if we should provide two line items (service + marketplace fee) for Acme procurement? Finance will need this for contract language and invoice reconciliation.

Lisa — if the committed rows line up with your $25–$40k/month target, tell us which committed tier you prefer and we’ll push a formal private-offer draft through Sophie.

Ben

Attachments: acme-private-offer-econ-draft.xlsx (updated)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Sophie Reed <sophie.reed@aws-partners.com>
Cc: Connor Obrien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-06 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: AWS Marketplace private offer — unit economics and next steps

Ben / Sophie — quick finance note: Redwood can support either approach but we prefer mapping as a single SKU on the customer AWS invoice (redwood_token_1M) with the gross list price visible. Finance will reconcile marketplace fees on our side; presenting a single invoice line reduces procurement friction for the customer. If Acme requires an explicit fee line for GL mapping, we can provide a supplemental breakdown for their records.

Next steps I recommend:
- Ben to confirm the committed tier (Lisa) so we can finalize gross price.
- Sophie to confirm SKU string and private-offer template acceptance.
- Redwood (Ben/Karthik) to generate a private-offer draft for Acme to sign; aim for a 2–3 business day turnaround after tier confirmation.

Karthik Iyer
Head of Revenue Operations, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

Subject: Request: Tailored demo + IT/security call — Orion Health

Hi Ben,

Thanks — I signed up through the self-serve flow and would like to schedule a tailored walkthrough for Orion Health. We're interested in latency characteristics for clinical note summarization and the VPC/private deployment options.

Important: Our IT and security team must attend (they'll validate network/egress and audit logging). Can you include an SE and a member of security/operations on the call?

Preferred window (ET): Oct 6–8 between 10:00–15:00 ET. If you need to propose earlier, we can make space on Oct 5 after 16:00 ET.

Attendees on our side: Alex Morgan (PM), Priya Sharma (IT lead), Jordan Li (Security).

Thanks,
Alex Morgan
Product Lead — Orion Health
alex.morgan@orionhealth.com

title: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Amy Park <amy@greenleafconsulting.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Team —

Following the engagement survey and the early signals from our managers, I’ve pulled together an initial at-risk roster and a proposed manager care cadence ahead of the Q4 review cycle. This is intentionally conservative — the goal is to get ahead of attrition where we can, and to ensure managers have a clear, confidential playbook for sensitive conversations.

Included:
- at_risk_roster_q32026.xlsx (roster with risk tiering, manager notes column)
- manager_playbook_q42026.pdf (draft manager script + escalation checklist)

Asks:
1) Managers (Laura, Evelyn, Marcus) — review the roster for your org and flag any inaccuracies or privacy concerns by EOD Monday (10/4). Mark rows as RED/YELLOW/GREEN.
2) Vivek — can you confirm thresholds where we should escalate to Compensation for retention credits?
3) Amy (Greenleaf) — please review the manager playbook and sensitive-case checklist; want confirmation that our approach is compliant with best practice for sensitive terminations/offboarding.

If anyone sees a likely immediate departure (verbal intent to leave, manager concerned), escalate to me directly and mark the row RED — we’ll treat those as urgent.

I’ve also added a shared Drive folder (link below) for secure documents. Use the secure transfer checklist — do not email attachments with PII.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
VP, People Operations
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: at_risk_roster_q32026.xlsx (XLSX, 24KB)]
[Attachment: manager_playbook_q42026.pdf (PDF, 520KB)]


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Aditya — thanks for pulling this together. Quick clarifying Qs:
- For RED-tier: are we expected to open retention conversations proactively (i.e., offer counter / early retention credits) or only after the manager confirms an employee has indicated intent to leave?
- Any guidance on timeline if we’re contemplating org changes that may trigger voluntary exits?

I’m worried about our busiest IC team (Platform) — I count 3 REDs and 2 YELLOWs there. If we escalate early, we should align on messaging and approval matrix so managers aren’t negotiating credits ad hoc.

I’ll review the roster today and annotate.

—Laura
Director, Engineering Managers
Redwood Inference


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Amy Park <amy@greenleafconsulting.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 13:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Thanks, Aditya. Short answers:
- RED-tier = immediate manager outreach + compensation pre-approval for small retention credits up to $Xk (I’ll share the thresholds). If manager reports \"verbal intent,\" treat as urgent and loop Compensation.
- YELLOW = manager cadence + targeted 1:1s over two weeks; escalate if signals persist.

I’m attaching the retention_thresholds.xlsx — this is the current guideline for approvals without exec signoff. Anything above the threshold needs a quick note to me + People Ops (Aditya) and finance.

Also: if there are planned org changes that could materially affect teams, we should block calendar time for team-level comms at least 48 hours before any public announcement where possible — that reduces rumor risk.

Vivek
Head of Product & Operations
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: retention_thresholds.xlsx (XLSX, 12KB)]


From: Amy Park <amy@greenleafconsulting.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Wells <sara.wells@hrlegal.co>
Date: Mon, 4 Oct 2026 08:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Thanks for including us. I reviewed the manager_playbook_q42026.pdf and have a few recommendations for handling sensitive cases:

1) Establish a two-person review for RED-tier offboarding (manager + People Ops) before any written offer/closure to ensure consistency.
2) Use a secure, audited channel for any PII — we recommend the Drive folder you linked but with access logged and limited to need-to-know.
3) Provide managers with a short script for voluntary exits vs. performance-related departures — helps reduce legal exposure.

I’ve attached a one-page Sensitive Case Checklist (sensitive_case_checklist.docx) with the operational steps and suggested evidence to collect for each case. Happy to run a 30-min drop-in for managers next week to walk through scenarios.

—Amy Park
Principal, Greenleaf Consulting
(attachment: sensitive_case_checklist.docx)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Amy Park <amy@greenleafconsulting.com>, Sara Wells <sara.wells@hrlegal.co>
Date: Mon, 4 Oct 2026 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Appreciate the fast reviews. Action items and owners:
- Laura / Marcus: finish roster verification and RED/YELLOW/GREEN marking by EOD Tuesday (10/5).
- Evelyn: run the three Platform REDs as priority — schedule 1:1s this week; if any verbal intent to leave, mark RED and escalate to me immediately.
- Vivek: confirm the approval threshold that can be applied without exec signoff (upload to the Drive folder).
- Amy / Sara: finalize the sensitive-case checklist and join the manager drop-in on Wed 10/6 @ 11:00 PT (I’ll send calendar invite).

I’ll consolidate final list on Wednesday morning and publish a short manager playbook update (one page) with the exact approval matrix and communication templates. If managers prefer, I can pre-author outreach language for them to use.

Note on secure transfers: use the Drive link and mark docs with the redwood-classification label; do not forward attachments externally without redaction.

I’ll set up a 30-min leader sync (Aditya, Vivek, Laura, Evelyn, Marcus) for Wed 10/6 at 10:00 PT to finalize thresholds.

Thanks — let’s keep this tight.
Aditya Rao
VP, People Operations
Redwood Inference

[Related link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Aq-ManagerCare-Q4]


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 Oct 2026 16:44:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Acknowledged — I’ve scheduled the three Platform 1:1s for Thursday and Friday. Heads-up: one of the REDs indicated they’re exploring other options and asked for a conversation about scope and growth; I’ll handle that track and confirm whether retention discussion is appropriate after the 1:1. I’ll update the roster immediately after each conversation and flag any immediate risks.

Evelyn
Senior Engineering Manager
Redwood Inference

title: Proposal: prompt log retention policy & output-rights amendment
preview:
From: Emma Liu <emma@brightedgehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightEdge Legal <legal@brightedgehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: prompt log retention policy & output-rights amendment

Ben / Laura —

Thanks for the productive call this morning. As discussed, BrightEdge Health needs a narrow amendment to the existing DPA so we can: (1) limit retention of raw prompt text and token-level logs to 30 days for PII-containing prompts unless we opt-in to longer retention for debugging; (2) add an explicit no-model-improvement clause for BrightEdge data (no training, fine-tuning, or model updates from our prompts/responses); and (3) clarify that BrightEdge retains ownership of customer-facing output artifacts generated from our prompts (reports, patient summaries) and that Redwood will not claim derivative IP.

I attached a first-pass redline to your DPA that adds the retention matrix and the explicit no-training language. Two specific asks from Legal:
- Can Redwood produce an attestation of your subprocessors that will have access to logs and confirm EU residency for backups if we mark the data as EU-only?
- We need a simple export of prompt/response records for audit within 30 days if requested. Is that feasible without exposing internal model internals?

Attaching: BrightEdge_DPA_amendment_redline.docx (redline) and a proposed log-retention matrix.

If you can confirm the technical feasibility and a timeline for the amendment (DocuSign preferred), we can route to procurement.

Thanks,
Emma Liu
Head of Partnerships
BrightEdge Health

[Attachment: BrightEdge_DPA_amendment_redline.docx (docx)]
[Attachment: log-retention-matrix.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Emma Liu <emma@brightedgehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: prompt log retention policy & output-rights amendment

Emma —

Thanks for the redline and matrix. Appreciate the clear asks. Quick answers up front: we can support a 30‑day default retention for raw prompts with a customer-controlled flag to retain longer for debugging; we can add an explicit no-training/no-fine-tune contractual clause; and we can add language confirming that customer-generated output artifacts remain the customer's IP.

Couple of clarifying items before I loop Legal/Privacy for final redline:
- When you say \"export of prompt/response records for audit within 30 days\", do you need a one-off bundle per audit request or continuous access via a secure S3 snapshot?
- For EU residency, are you expecting primary storage+backups to remain in EU regions, or is EU residency limited to backups only?

Looping in Karthik for technical/legal confirmation and Laura for DPA routing. We can prepare a tracked DocuSign amendment once we confirm the export mechanism and subprocessor attestations.

Next steps: Karthik / Laura, can you review Emma's redline and confirm feasibility and an estimated timeline?

Cheers,
Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0147

[Inline: original message quoted below]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Emma Liu <emma@brightedgehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 13:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: prompt log retention policy & output-rights amendment

Emma / Ben —

Technical and compliance notes from Privacy/Legal side:

1) Retention: we can implement a tenant-level retention policy that deletes raw prompt text and token logs after 30 days. We maintain two separate retention classes: \"raw prompts\" and \"derived telemetry\" (latency, token counts). We can surface a customer control to extend retention to 90 days for debugging; that would require an explicit opt-in UI toggle and an audit trail.

2) No-training clause: we can accept a contractual no-model-improvement commitment limited to BrightEdge-provided prompts and responses processed via your account. We will add a provision excluding BrightEdge content from any training/fine-tuning pipelines and will ensure any analytics that involve model updates are run on anonymized/aggregated data not tied to customer prompts.

3) Export/audit delivery: We can provide a secure, time-limited S3 export bundle (encrypted) containing redacted prompt/response pairs for audit within 30 days of request. The bundle will exclude model weights and internal state. If you need continuous access, we can provide a read-only S3 snapshot with access logs; that adds a small monthly fee.

4) Subprocessors & EU residency: our current subprocessors list is available in the vendor portal. title: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates
preview:
From: Tom Hayes <tom.hayes@acmehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates

Hi Ava / Grace,

We're putting together a short enablement pack for our field engineering team so they can triage and escalate cost & latency incidents against Redwood endpoints. Can you help with a lightweight \"quickstart\" that includes:
- Per-route alert templates (examples with suggested thresholds)
- A compact dashboard pack (1-page per-route view) showing cost breakdown, tail latency percentiles, and recent regressions
- A short runbook for on-call and a one-page escalation/approval flow for changes to thresholds

Ideal target: a 1-hour handoff + docs and a set of templates we can reuse across customers. We also attached our intake form (acme-request-form.pdf) with expected traffic profiles.

Two quick questions that will inform templates:
1) Do you have canonical threshold guidance for 95th/99th latency buckets vs baseline?
2) Is there an opinionated cost-spike rule (e.g., % week-over-week) you recommend?

Thanks — appreciate any off-the-shelf material we can adapt.

Tom Hayes
Principal SRE, Acme Health
attachments: acme-request-form.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Hayes <tom.hayes@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates

Hi Tom — thanks for the context and the intake form. This is exactly the kind of lightweight kit we can assemble. Proposed scope for the quickstart (deliverables and owners):

1) Quickstart doc (rdw-observability-quickstart-outline.md) — overview, one-page runbook, handoff checklist (owner: me)
2) Alert templates (sample-alert-templates.json) — per-route templates for latency, error rate, and spend spikes (owner: Kimberly)
3) Dashboard pack — a 1-page per-route view with: 95th/99th latency, cost per 1k tokens, token-pathway breakdown, and recent regressions (owner: Marcus)
4) Approval & rollback flow — short decision tree and required approvers for changing thresholds (owner: Product / Legal as needed)

A couple of asks from our side so we don't overfit the templates:
- Can you confirm the 90/95/99 traffic percentiles and a representative peak qps from your intake?
- Any routes you consider "high-cost" or special (e.g., long-context retrieval, multimodal) so we can add callouts?

I'll attach the outline and a starter JSON for alert templates to iterate. Suggest a 30-min kickoff next week to align on acceptance criteria.

Best,
Ava Chen
Product Manager, Observability — Redwood Inference
attachments: rdw-observability-quickstart-outline.md

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates

+1 on Ava's scope. A few practical suggestions from the ops side:

- Latency thresholds: we typically start with baseline-based thresholds (baseline = 7d median). Suggested starters: 95th > baseline * 2 OR absolute > 1200ms --> trigger page; 99th > baseline * 3 OR > 2000ms -> page + start incident runbook.
- Cost spike: 30% WoW increase for a route or 2x typical per-1k-token cost in 1h window -> paging alert. Keep a lower-severity (email) alert at 15% WoW for ops review.
- Canary set: include 5 representative routes (low, mid, high cost; short and long context) so dashboards show sensible distributions.

I'll drop a folder in Drive with example templates: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ObsTemplatesExample — contains a dashboard JSON and alert snippets we use internally.

Kimberly Park
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-03T14:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates

Thanks — adding a couple of things we should include in acceptance criteria for the field kit:

- Dashboard tile: per-route cost breakdown (token compute vs model size vs I/O) + 95/99 latency percentiles for last 1h/24h/7d. Tile should link to trace/excerpt playback for the top 3 call chains in the time window.
- Sampling guidance: default 1% full-trace sampling, but allow 100% sampling for canary routes for 24h during rollouts.
- Regression detection: automated diff on 95th latency week-over-week with a configurable tolerance (start at 15%).
- Ownership & approvals: any change to a route's paging threshold must include a sign-off from Product (product-counsel@redwood.com) if it reduces alert sensitivity for a customer-facing SLA.

I'll create a short Jira (SUP-1294) to track the playbook work and link the templates.

— Marcus
Principal Observability Engineer, Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lin <sarah.lin@atlasedge.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-03T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick follow-up & next steps (procurement path)

Hi Sarah —

Great to meet earlier — thanks for the time and for walking through the integration goals with AtlasEdge. Per our call, I wanted to summarize the simplest paths to get started and confirm what your procurement team prefers so we can hand off the exact paperwork.

Summary of options (short):
- Marketplace (AWS/GCP): fastest for procurement-managed lists, purchase via marketplace listing; limited to market terms and may be immediate after invoice.
- Corporate PO: standard vendor setup + signed vendor packet + W-9/W-8 as applicable; net30 typical but we can support other terms if needed.
- Corporate card: for small POs or pilot spend, quick but may require a card holder to create subscription.

Action items from my side:
1) If you want PO route, I’ll send our vendor packet and W-9 and loop finance (bank instructions) — Marissa will help with vendor setup.
2) If you want marketplace, confirm which marketplace (AWS Marketplace or Google Cloud Marketplace) and we’ll confirm listing readiness.
3) If you need any legal redlines, please flag them and I’ll connect our contracts lead.

Can you confirm which path AtlasEdge prefers and any internal forms we should fill out (vendor intake portal link, supplier questionnaire)? If you prefer PO, what’s your typical turnaround and who should receive the packet?

I’ve attached a one-page onboarding checklist (attachment stub) for quick reference — I’ll follow up with the full packet once you confirm.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0198

title: Renewal alignment + multi-window upsell orchestration
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lara Chen <lara.chen@bluehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Renewal planning: draft multi-window upsell and co-terming options

Hi Lara —

Thanks again for the conversation earlier this week. Attaching a first-pass proposal that aims to do three things for BlueHealth:

- Align contract windows across your US and EU subsidiaries (co-terming) so we can simplify billing and true-up cadence.
- Offer a 2-year staged ramp that preserves your per-token pricing through predictable commits while letting you burst capacity during seasonal spikes.
- Keep auto-renew as opt-in for legal/procurement flexibility, with an explicit 60-day notice window and a canary period for price changes.

High-level option included in the PDF: a two-year committed plan that phases in additional Reserved (Dedicated) capacity at quarter boundaries (Q1: baseline, Q3: +25% burst pool, Q5: +50%). We call out expected latency SLOs per pool and a credit schedule for missed SLOs.

As requested, I’ve cc’d Lauren to include billing cadence and Neha will be on standby for contract/legal questions once you signal which option you prefer. Can we aim to lock option selection by Oct 7 to hit procurement deadlines on your side? If helpful, I can convert the draft to a mutual action plan (MAP) with milestone owners and DocuSign-ready redlines.

Attachments: 20261001-ben_proposal_draft.pdf

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | (415) 555-0198

1) Procurement timeline: we have an internal cutover to a new fiscal vendor on Oct 20, so we need commercial terms signed by Oct 15 to avoid a manual PO gap.
2) Co-terming: we like the idea but need per-subsidiary billing visibility. Can you confirm that invoices will still show usage by legal entity while honoring a single contract boundary?
3) Auto-renew: legal prefers auto-renew disabled by default; we can accept opt-in with an explicit clause if we get extended notice and a floor on price increases.

Also attaching our procurement timeline so you can see required dates. We’ll need a redline-ready contract and an initial SOW that maps the ramp schedule to capacity milestones.

Attachments: BlueHealth_procurement_timeline.xlsx

—Lara
Lara Chen
Head of Platform Ops | BlueHealth Systems
lara.chen@bluehealth.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lara Chen <lara.chen@bluehealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T10:08:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal planning: draft multi-window upsell and co-terming options

Lara —

Appreciate the clarity on procurement dates. Quick answers, plus a proposed next step: 

- Billing & co-terming: Yes — we can maintain per-entity usage breakout on the invoice while applying a single master agreement with synchronized renewal date. That will preserve your internal cost allocations while simplifying the legal window.
- Auto-renew: We’ll mark auto-renew as opt-in. I propose this language: \"Auto-renewal may be enabled upon mutual written consent no earlier than 30 days prior to renewal; any price increase tied to auto-renew shall be capped at 8% year-over-year during the term.\" Happy to run that by Neha for legal placement.

Proposed MAP (high level):
- Oct 2: Confirm preferred commercial option between A (2-year staged ramp) or B (3-year smoothing ladder). Owner: Lara
- Oct 5: Redwood to provide redline-ready contract and SOW (Ben + Neha)
- Oct 9: BlueHealth legal & procurement initial review
- Oct 15: Sign commercial terms / route for PO

Pricing mechanics (summary):
- Option A (2-yr, staged): Year 1 baseline commit + 25% burst pool available after Q3; Year 2 baseline uplift + 50% burst; blended effective unit rate locked for committed baseline.
- Optional early-upgrade credit: apply remaining committed credits pro-rata if BlueHealth converts burst usage into baseline earlier than scheduled.

I’ve uploaded an editable MAP and a Drive folder with a redline template: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/0AexampleDriveId/renewal-pack. If you confirm Option A today, I’ll deliver the redline contract and SOW by COB Oct 5.

Ben

title: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs
preview:
From: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Ben — thanks for taking this on. Attached is a short requirements doc from our infra team (latency targets, concurrency expectations, and a note on cold-start tolerance). High-level asks from Atlas Health: 

- End-to-end P50 token latency <= 200ms for the primary clinical summarization flow (avg prompt length ~120 tokens).
- Support concurrent interactive sessions for ~50 clinicians with occasional spikes to 120.
- TTFT (time to first token) under 220ms for cold requests 95% of the time.
- Deterministic output quality: we will run a 500-example eval set (examples attached in the RFP).

We're trying to fold Redwood's benchmarking and variant comparisons into the MAP so procurement can sign off quickly. Can you propose a concrete cadence (what you will measure, when, and what pass/fail criteria you expect) and a recommended production variant (or shortlist) that balances cost and quality?

Attachments: ATLAS_RFP_requirements.pdf (5 pages)

Thanks,
Sarah Nguyen
Program Manager, Platform Reliability
Atlas Health


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
Cc: John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Sarah — appreciate the clarity. Quick reply with a proposed MAP-integrated benchmarking plan and timeline. I'll loop in Priya for the technical bench run and Karthik for commercial constraints. Summary below (full MAP doc attached).

Proposed cadence (2-week sprint view):
- Week 0 (Today): Align on eval set (you mentioned 500 examples) and SLO definitions.
- Week 1: Redwood runs parallel microbenchmarks (latency/TTFT) across 3 candidate variants; collect throughput/concurrency profiles.
- Week 2: Quality eval (500 examples) and combined perf-quality tradeoff analysis + cost model.
- Week 2 end: MAP deliverable: go/no-go recommendation + canary rollout plan.

Pass/fail criteria (suggested):
- Latency: P50 token latency <= 200ms and P95 <= 400ms for interactive flows at target concurrency.
- TTFT: median <= 160ms, 95th <= 220ms.
- Quality: automated metrics (ROUGE/BERTScore or custom metrics) not worse than your baseline by > 2% and human spot-checks acceptable.
- Concurrency: baseline sustain at 50 sessions with <10% latency degradation; spike mode to 120 with degraded but available behavior + fallback variant if exhausted.

Attachments: mutual_action_plan_v3.docx, redwood_variant_summary.pdf

Action items:
1) Priya to confirm benchmark config and test harness.
2) Atlas to share the 500 example eval set and baseline metrics.
3) Karthik to provide estimated unit economics per variant (day 1 and steady state).

Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0182


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>, Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T15:48:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Folks — attaching a short results summary after our microbench run (attached CSV has full traces). Key notes from the lab run (all tests run on m60-equivalent infra, synthetic prompt distribution matched to your 120-token median):

Candidate variants tested:
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f (quantized fast path) — optimized for latency
- rw-gpt-13b (mixed precision) — balanced quality/throughput
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365 (higher fidelity quantized) — higher quality, higher cost

Microbench highlights (single-VM steady concurrency profile):
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f: P50 token latency 85ms; TTFT median 90ms; sustain throughput 320 tok/s; handles 60 concurrent user sessions (interactive) before tail latency +30%.
- rw-gpt-13b: P50 token latency 140ms; TTFT median 150ms; sustain throughput 210 tok/s; handles 50 sessions with <10% degradation.
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365: P50 token latency 210ms; TTFT median 230ms; sustain throughput 150 tok/s; good quality on our eval set but TTFT exceeds your stated 220ms threshold at cold start for 95th percentile.

Quality spot-check (initial):
- On your 500-example holdout: rw-gpt-13b and rw-gpt-13b-q8_365 outperform rw-gpt-7b-q4f on clinical fidelity metrics; difference between 13b and 13b-q8_365 is smaller (~1.2% on our BERTScore proxy) but non-zero on edge-case factuality checks. Full CSV contains per-example scores.

Recommendations (shortlist):
- If TTFT and latency are hard SLOs: rw-gpt-7b-q4f as primary with rw-gpt-13b as fallback for high-quality batch tasks (or selective routing for complex prompts).
- If quality is prioritized and TTFT can be mitigated with warm caches: rw-gpt-13b (or q8 variant) but plan on pre-warming and a higher cost bucket.

Next steps:
- I will run the 500-example end-to-end eval tomorrow and upload atlas_map_benchmark_results_v1.csv to the Drive link.
- Evelyn: can you advise on canary shard size and rollback SLI?

Priya Natarajan
Platform Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com


From: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Thanks — this is exactly the level of detail we needed. A couple quick clarifications before we lock the MAP: 

- For rw-gpt-7b-q4f, what is the expected cost delta vs rw-gpt-13b per 1M tokens (ballpark)? We need that for procurement.
- On the canary, we want progressive rollout across 3 hospital clusters; can you propose shard percentages and SLI thresholds for auto-rollback?

Also, we care about deterministic behavior for clinical summaries (we will accept some latency increase if it meaningfully reduces hallucinations). If rw-gpt-13b-q8 shows only ~1% quality improvement but doubles cost, we will favor the 7b option. Please loop Karthik for pricing and Evelyn for rollout specifics.

Sarah


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-03T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Sarah — copying high-level commercial guidance and a proposed close timeline so procurement has clarity.

Pricing (ballpark per 1M tokens, USD):
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f: $220 / 1M tokens (optimized quantized footprint)
- rw-gpt-13b: $480 / 1M tokens
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365: $900 / 1M tokens (higher infra and memory footprint)

Recommended commercial approach:
- Start with rw-gpt-7b-q4f for production canary + selective routing to rw-gpt-13b for complex prompts. This reduces unit cost while preserving quality where it matters.
- MAP stages:
  * Week A (benchmarks & quality) — current (complete).
  * Week B (canary rollout across cluster-1 at 5% traffic, monitor 72 hours).
  * Week C (expand to cluster-2 at 20% if SLI stable).
  * Week D (full migration across clusters 3+ if green).

Suggested canary shard sizes and SLI thresholds:
- Canary shards: 5% -> 20% -> 50% -> 100% across three cluster groups.
- SLIs for auto-rollback: P50 latency increase > 25% or error rate > 1.5% or clinical-fidelity degradation on sampling > 3% vs baseline.

Proposed close timeline (target):
- 2026-10-10: Final MAP and commercial play accepted by Atlas procurement.
- 2026-10-17: Canary start (cluster-1).
- 2026-10-31: Full production if canary green.

HubSpot reference: HS-987654 (I will update with the final quote + contract redlines).

Karthik Iyer
Director, Customer Solutions | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

- On your 500-example holdout: rw-gpt-13b and rw-gpt-13b-q8_365 outperform rw-gpt-7b-q4f on clinical fidelity metrics; difference between 13b and 13b-q8_365 is smaller (~1.2% on our BERTScore proxy) but non-zero on edge-case factuality checks. Full CSV contains per-example scores.

Recommendations (shortlist):
- If TTFT and latency are hard SLOs: rw-gpt-7b-q4f as primary with rw-gpt-13b as fallback for high-quality batch tasks (or selective routing for complex prompts).
- If quality is prioritized and TTFT can be mitigated with warm caches: rw-gpt-13b (or q8 variant) but plan on pre-warming and a higher cost bucket.

Next steps:
- I will run the 500-example end-to-end eval tomorrow and upload atlas_map_benchmark_results_v1.csv to the Drive link.
- Evelyn: can you advise on canary shard size and rollback SLI?

Priya Natarajan
Platform Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Thanks — this is exactly the level of detail we needed. A couple quick clarifications before we lock the MAP: 

- For rw-gpt-7b-q4f, what is the expected cost delta vs rw-gpt-13b per 1M tokens (ballpark)? We need that for procurement.
- On the canary, we want progressive rollout across 3 hospital clusters; can you propose shard percentages and SLI thresholds for auto-rollback?

Also, we care about deterministic behavior for clinical summaries (we will accept some latency increase if it meaningfully reduces hallucinations). If rw-gpt-13b-q8 shows only ~1% quality improvement but doubles cost, we will favor the 7b option. Please loop Karthik for pricing and Evelyn for rollout specifics.

Sarah


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-03T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Sarah — copying high-level commercial guidance and a proposed close timeline so procurement has clarity.

Pricing (ballpark per 1M tokens, USD):
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f: $220 / 1M tokens (optimized quantized footprint)
- rw-gpt-13b: $480 / 1M tokens
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365: $900 / 1M tokens (higher infra and memory footprint)

Recommended commercial approach:
- Start with rw-gpt-7b-q4f for production canary + selective routing to rw-gpt-13b for complex prompts. This reduces unit cost while preserving quality where it matters.
- MAP stages:
  * Week A (benchmarks & quality) — current (complete).
  * Week B (canary rollout across cluster-1 at 5% traffic, monitor 72 hours).
  * Week C (expand to cluster-2 at 20% if SLI stable).
  * Week D (full migration across clusters 3+ if green).

Suggested canary shard sizes and SLI thresholds:
- Canary shards: 5% -> 20% -> 50% -> 100% across three cluster groups.
- SLIs for auto-rollback: P50 latency increase > 25% or error rate > 1.5% or clinical-fidelity degradation on sampling > 3% vs baseline.

Proposed close timeline (target):
- 2026-10-10: Final MAP and commercial play accepted by Atlas procurement.
- 2026-10-17: Canary start (cluster-1).
- 2026-10-31: Full production if canary green.

HubSpot reference: HS-987654 (I will update with the final quote + contract redlines).
