title: Urgent: Stratos connector failing on customer digest exports — SLA action plan
preview:
From: Elena Park <elena.park@stratos.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 3 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Stratos connector failing on customer digest exports

Hi Ava,

We're seeing a sudden spike in failures from the Stratos<>Redwood connector that powers daily digest exports for a set of our customers. The first alert came in at ~09:05 PDT and is impacting NordicHealth and two other customers — exports are queuing and timing out with a 502 from your API: POST /v1/ingest/stratos-export.

Error snippet (attached): error_trace.log — stack shows repeated HTTP 502 from redwood-prod behind the gateway. We've tried a restart on our side and reproduced the problem from two different cluster regions.

Can your on-call take a look? This is paginating into customers and will breach our SLO for export completion (we're already receiving customer escalations). If you prefer, we can stand up a quick war room.

Attachments: error_trace.log (5.2 KB)

Thanks,
Elena Park
Partner Ops, Stratos Systems
elena.park@stratos.io


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@stratos.io>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 3 Oct 2026 09:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Stratos connector failing on customer digest exports

Elena — thanks for the heads up and the trace. We're on it.

Quick plan (mutual action plan):
- Immediate: Stand up an incident war room (proposed Zoom link listed below).
- Triage: We need: 1) full request/response logs (presigned S3 link preferred), 2) Stratos config manifest for the connector, 3) customer job IDs for impacted exports.
- Mitigation (short window): We'll route Stratos traffic to a compatible fallback model variant (redwood-compat-v2-ql4) while we isolate the root cause. This should keep packets flowing with slightly higher CPU but lower error rate.
- Follow-up: Postmortem + permanent fix + SLA credit conversation if required.

Jonas — can you lead the kernel/serving check (KV cache flush, recent kernel swaps)? Stephanie — can you own the communication to NordicHealth and keep them in the loop on the timeline?

I'll join the war room in 5. Zoom link: https://zoom.us/j/9876543210?pwd=warroom

Thanks — attaching our quick fallback playbook for your ops team: redwood-fallback-playbook.pdf

—
Ava Chen
Senior Partner Engineering, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0172


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>, Elena Park <elena.park@stratos.io>
Date: Sat, 3 Oct 2026 09:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Stratos connector failing on customer digest exports

On Sat, Oct 3, 2026 at 09:27, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Elena — thanks for the heads up and the trace. We're on it.

I've started a quick health sweep of the serving fleet and router. Early notes: a subset of requests hitting redwood-prod are returning upstream 502s tied to a recently deployed kernel change that affects attention batching in long-sequence paths. Hypothesis: connector request shape triggers a path that exercises a newer kernel with lower stability under specific sequence-length + batch combos.

Plan of action:
- Immediately re-route Stratos calls to redwood-compat-v2-ql4 (will be done in config in ~10m).
- Flush KV cache shards for the impacted host groups.
- If re-route + cache flush does not reduce error rate to <1% within 30m, escalate to infra rollback (we have a safe fallback snapshot).

I'll post updates in the war room.

Thanks,
Jonas Weber
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Elena Park <elena.park@stratos.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marco Li <marco.li@stratos.io>
Date: Sat, 3 Oct 2026 09:52:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Stratos connector failing on customer digest exports

Ava / team — joining the war room now. I've uploaded full request samples and one combined gateway trace to S3: https://s3.stratos.io/presigned/stratos-logs/20261003/export-12345.log (expires in 24h). Also attached a small export_error_snippet.txt that shows the failing payload.

Two customers reproduced the issue (NordicHealth and MedScale). Impact: missed overnight export cycles, customers are in contact with our account teams. We appreciate the quick reroute option.

Elena

Attachments: export_error_snippet.txt (1.1 KB)


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@stratos.io>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 3 Oct 2026 13:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Stratos connector failing on customer digest exports — SLA action plan (resolved)


Hi all,

Update from the war room and remediation timeline:
- 12:42 PDT: We routed Stratos traffic to redwood-compat-v2-ql4 and flushed KV cache shards for the impacted hosts. Error rate dropped from ~6% to 0.4% in the next 12 minutes.
- 12:55 PDT: Deployed a targeted config patch that avoids the new attention batching path for connector shaped requests.
- 13:00 PDT: Verified successful exports for the two impacted customers (NordicHealth, MedScale).

Next steps:
- We'll run a full regression test suite against the staging snapshot that caused the regression and open an infra rollback ticket INFRA-1589 if we see broader impact.
- Postmortem: I'll draft an incident postmortem with timelines and proposed SLA credits by EOD Monday and share the Drive link: https://drive.redwood.ai/Shared/Stratos/STRATO-342-postmortem
- Ops alignment: recommend Stratos enable short-term retry/backoff on export job submissions for 24 hours while we monitor.

Can you confirm from Stratos and from NordicHealth that exports look healthy on your side? If all good, we'll close this thread and follow-up with the postmortem.

Thanks for the quick collaboration — attaching the fallback playbook we referenced: redwood-fallback-playbook.pdf

—
Ava Chen
Senior Partner Engineering, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0172

title: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

From: Prof. Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@unibo.it>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Oak Ridge BioLab <datalab@oakridge.edu>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 15:12:00 +0200
Subject: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

Hello Ava,

I'm Elena Rossi, PI for the Computational Biology lab at the University of Bologna. We're preparing a small pilot (30 participants) using a Redwood-hosted model for de-identified symptom synthesis research. Our internal IRB process has taken longer than expected due to a new data minimization clause; IRB will likely be formally approved in ~6 weeks.

We were wondering whether Redwood can grant a provisional compute subsidy / trial-top up so we can complete onboarding and internal evaluation while we await the final IRB. We're requesting 6,000 inference credits or equivalent for 90 days so our students can finish integration testing and the privacy-preserving pre-processing pipeline.

Attached: IRB_provisional_notice_unibo.pdf (signed provisional note from the ethics office) and project_scope_and_datasets.xlsx (high level - all data will be synthetic or fully anonymized).

Happy to provide any additional documentation (data handling SOPs, K-anonymity summary). Our timeline is tight: kickoff workshop on Oct 26 — if you can support provisioning before then, that would be ideal.

Best,
Prof. Elena Rossi
Department of Computational Biology
University of Bologna

Attachment: IRB_provisional_notice_unibo.pdf (pdf)
Attachment: project_scope_and_datasets.xlsx (xlsx)

title: Tiered reserve / sprint plan — negotiation on phased Dedicated ramp
preview:
From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@auroraai.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@auroraai.com
Date: 2026-09-27T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Aurora — Dedicated committed capacity: ramp & prepay options

Camila,

Thanks for the demo last week — our infra team is excited about Redwood's runtime and the deterministic SLOs you showed. We're evaluating a 3-year reserved plan for Dedicated capacity to support our inference fleet (vision+chat). Before we loop legal: two asks so we can do an internal cost model.

1) Can you share a concrete ramp schedule example for a phased onramp (GPU count and TPM footprint) with pricing per tranche and term discounts for 3 years? We want to see both an even-quarter ramp and a frontloaded ramp.
2) What's the discount delta if we do 100% annual prepay vs 60% upfront + quarterly settlements? We have capital restrictions but want price predictability.

If you can send a short term sheet + a simple proforma (month-by-month) we'll run numbers this week. CCed contracts for procurement context.

Thanks — Sam
Sam Patel
Head of Infrastructure, Aurora AI


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@auroraai.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, contracts@auroraai.com
Date: 2026-09-28T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Aurora — Dedicated committed capacity: ramp & prepay options

Thanks Sam — appreciate the clarity. Attached is a short term sheet (Aurora_Dedicated_Term_Sheet_v1.pdf) and a simple month-by-month proforma (ramp-proforma-3yr.xlsx). High-level summary below so you can share with finance/infra quickly.

Option A — Phased stair-step (even-quarter ramp)
- 3-year term, annual prepay: 18% off list on committed GPU-hours + 12% off on overflow usage
- Ramp: Q1 20% capacity, Q2 40%, Q3 70%, Q4 100% (relative to target baseline)
- Billing: annual prepay (full year at T0) or 60% upfront + 40% yearly (we show both scenarios)

Option B — Frontloaded sprint (higher early capacity)
- 3-year term, annual prepay: 22% off list on committed GPU-hours + 10% overflow
- Ramp: Q1 50%, Q2 80%, Q3 100%
- Billing: to support frontload we recommend 70% upfront to secure capacity in Q1; pricing assumes higher utilization guarantee

Notes / trade-offs:
- We can tie additional discounting to a ramp SLT: if Aurora commits to reaching 90% of target by month 9, we increase the effective discount by 2% across years 2-3.
- We model TPM as throughput units; the proforma maps TPM to estimated GPU-hrs based on your provided profile.

Next steps: Ben (cc) will run a margin/backstop check and Laura can help with procurement terms. If this looks directionally right I'll get legal a redlineable term sheet.

Cheers,
Camila Reyes
Principal Sales Manager — Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@auroraai.com>
Cc: contracts@auroraai.com
Date: 2026-09-29T15:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Aurora — Dedicated committed capacity: ramp & prepay options

Quick internal note for transparency (copying Aurora so we're aligned):
Camila's two options are the pragmatic ones. A couple of pieces Aurora should expect from our side if we move to a frontloaded sprint:
- We need a predictable utilization profile (we used the TPM curves you shared in the model) — if real usage deviates materially, we'll discuss reconciliation language (monthly true-ups or crediting).
- For the 70% upfront ask on the sprint: operationally that secures the specific GPU pool for Q1. We can offer a 30% refundable credit if Aurora fails to spin up the expected capacity within the first 120 days (gives your CFO downside protection).

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Strategic Accounts — Redwood Inference


From: Sam Patel <sam.patel@auroraai.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, contracts@auroraai.com
Date: 2026-10-01T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Aurora — Dedicated committed capacity: ramp & prepay options

Camila / Ben — thanks for the clear options. A few follow-ups from procurement and finance after reviewing the proforma:

1) Our preferred structure is a 3-year term with annual prepay (one invoice per year). We cannot do 70% upfront in cash for year 1; 60% upfront + 40% invoiced within 90 days is doable.
2) We like the stair-step ramp (even-quarter) but we'd prefer the step timing: Month 1: 15%, Month 4: 35%, Month 7: 65%, Month 10: 100% — a slightly slower start than Option A. Can Redwood adjust discount tiers for this cadence?
3) We want a clearer exit-protection clause if SLA miss exceeds 2% latency degradation for three consecutive months (we'll have legal redlines on that).

If you can send a counter with: adjusted discount schedule for the requested cadence, revised billing (60% upfront + 40% by day 90), and the refundable credit language spelled out, we can move to legal review.

Sam


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@auroraai.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, contracts@auroraai.com
Date: 2026-10-02T16:43:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Aurora — Dedicated committed capacity: ramp & prepay options

Sam — appreciate the specific asks. We've updated the proforma and the term sheet with your cadence and billing preference. Summary of our counter: 

- Term: 3 years, annual invoice cadence.
- Billing: 60% due at signature, remaining 40% net 90 days.
- Ramp cadence (Aurora request): Month 1 15%, Month 4 35%, Month 7 65%, Month 10 100%.
- Discounting: 19% off list on committed GPU-hours year 1 under the above cadence, 20% year 2, 21% year 3. (This blends our stair-step and utilization uplift — full details in the updated term sheet attachment.)
- Refundable protection: 30% capacity credit refundable if Aurora does not provision >=80% of reserved capacity by day 120; trigger mechanics in the draft.
- SLA exit protection: we'll accept a material SLA remedy clause — specifics to be negotiated by legal but we won't block an exit right if latency is >2% beyond agreed SLO for three consecutive months.

I've uploaded a redline-ready term sheet and a Docusign prep link for signatures when you're ready (link in related_links). Laura — can your team own configuring the DocuSign envelope and coordinating legal redlines? Ben and I will stand by for procurement questions.

If Aurora is aligned, I'll ask legal to push a redline and then we can hand it to contracts.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this quickly.

Camila Reyes

title: FluxHealth pricing path: staged billing + midcycle uplift discussion
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-26T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Claire — great talking earlier. Per our call, FluxHealth is looking to add ~30% extra committed capacity mid-contract and prefers not to move the renewal date. Two things I want to propose so we can get legal and finance aligned quickly:

1) Staged billing: split the incremental uplift across 3 invoices over the remaining 9 months (monthly amortization on the uplift portion) so cashflow stays predictable on your side.

2) Discount ladder: a blended uplift discount of 8% on the additional QPS if you commit to the staged payment schedule, or 5% if you prefer a one-time midcycle invoice.

I've attached a draft SOW and a sample invoice proposal (staged schedule). If you can confirm preferred path (split vs one-off) and if procurement needs PO or set of statements, we'll route the right paperwork.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0127

[Attachment: Flux_SOW_v3.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: Invoice_Proposal_Staged.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-27T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Ben — thanks, this is helpful. A few clarifying items from procurement and finance:

• We prefer staged billing but need a clear invoice schedule we can attach to the PO. Can the staged invoices be tied to calendar months (e.g., Oct/Nov/Dec) instead of equal monthly amortization?
• Is the blended uplift discount of 8% applied only to the incremental capacity, or to total committed run rate after the top-up?
• Our renewal still runs to 2027-03-31 — is there any operational impact if the new capacity is co-termed to the existing renewal (i.e., prorated to end-of-March)?

Also looping in Eli (procurement) for PO timing.

—Claire
Claire Dunn
Director, Infrastructure Procurement
FluxHealth
claire@fluxhealth.com

[Procurement cc: Eli Navarro <eli@fluxhealth.com>]

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>, Eli Navarro <eli@fluxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-28T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Claire / Eli — answers inline: 

• Calendar months OK. We can create three invoices aligned to Oct/Nov/Dec (first invoice the month after signature). We'll include invoice lines referencing the PO number for matching.
• Discount applies only to the incremental capacity (the uplift portion). Base commitment pricing stays as-is.
• Co-term: we can pro-rate the added capacity to your renewal date and present two options in the SOW: (A) keep uplift on its own 12-month clock (no co-term), or (B) co-term and prorate the added capacity to 2027-03-31. Option B reduces the effective monthly billing in the short term but results in a higher renewal baseline next year.

Attached a small CSV that shows the prorate math for the co-term option; please review and tell me which you prefer. If you choose co-term, legal will prepare a short amendment rather than a full SOW replacement.

Next steps: pick staged vs one-off and whether you want co-term. I can copy the final invoice schedule into the PO attachment.

Ben
[Attachment: co-term_timing_and_prorate.csv (csv)]

To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-29T11:20:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Internal note — FluxHealth prefers staged (calendar months) and is leaning toward co-term to simplify next-year budgeting. Operational items to flag if we co-term:

• KV-cache and model routing changes: engineering needs a 7-day lead to adjust prefix caching if we enable additional dedicated pools mid-month.
• Billing system: finance needs the PO + signed amendment at least 5 business days before the first staged invoice.
• SLO impact: Marcus, can ops confirm whether adding 30% committed capacity to their existing pool triggers a different placement policy (different GPU class)? If yes, include a short clause about placement parity.

I recommend we provide an appendix that shows both pro-forma outcomes (co-term vs 12-month ramp) with clear renewal impact. I'll draft the appendix once Ben confirms pricing details.

Questions for leadership: Lauren, can you greenlight up to 10% for this midcycle uplift for FluxHealth (Strategic, 3yr pipeline)? Marcus, ops confirmation on placement parity would be helpful to avoid SLA exceptions. If approved, I will send a short amendment for signature and schedule the Oct invoice to go out 10/15.

Thanks — trying to keep it tight on timing.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

title: Final decision: Sr Backend Eng (Samantha Liu) — band & offer evaluation
preview:
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Recommendation: Sr Backend — Samantha Liu (band + offer)

Team,

Quick recap from the panel and my recommendation for REQ-2134. We completed the loop with Samantha yesterday (loop: system design + coding + culture). Highlights:
- Strong distributed systems reasoning, good trade-off awareness.
- Coding review showed some gaps in idiomatic Go (minor).
- Prefers low-latency infra work; aligns with team roadmap.

Comp ask: $215k base + equity (Samantha stated current comp ~ $200k base). Our posted band for the role is $160k–$200k (Band 5). After calibration with compensation, Samantha is outside our top-of-band by ~7.5%.

Recommendation: No offer at current band. Options:
1) Make an exception above Band 5 (requires Exec approval + SR compensation sign-off).
2) Keep to band, extend a counter within Band 5 (risk candidate decline).
3) No hire — re-open req for candidate pool or reclassify to Band 6 for senior infra role.

My read: strong potential but not a clear match for a band exception given current headcount and comp guardrails. I propose we close this candidate as a "no hire" and re-evaluate headcount at the next planning cadence.

Attached: resume + consolidated interviewer notes.

-Rishi
Rishi Malhotra
Engineering Manager, Platform
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- samantha_liu_resume.pdf
- interview_panel_notes.docx

- If we reclass to Band 6, do we have a backfill slot or would that be additive to our commit? (Concern: Q4 headcount freeze for non-critical roles.)
- Any flagged concerns on culture fit that would push us away even if comp were approved?

- No culture red flags from the panel (positive collaboration examples noted), so the decision is purely comp/headcount.

Decision: Close candidate as No Hire for REQ-2134. Action items:

- Laura, please flag REQ-2134 for reclassification discussion in the Oct 14 hiring sync if the team needs a senior slot.

Subject: RE: Final decision: Sr Backend Eng (Samantha Liu) — band & offer evaluation

Hi Samantha,

Thank you for interviewing with Redwood Inference over the last few weeks. We enjoyed learning about your background and the systems work you led at your current company. After careful consideration with the interview panel, we will not be extending an offer at this time. The decision was driven by compensation band alignment and our current headcount constraints — not a reflection of your technical strengths.

Panel feedback (high level):
- Distributed systems design: strong problem framing and tradeoffs.
- Coding: solid, with some opportunities to tighten Go idioms and microservice error handling.
- Collaboration: very positive; interviewers noted clear communication.

If you're open, we'd like to keep you in our pipeline for senior infra roles that match your comp expectations. I'll mark you as "keep warm" and reach out if a better-aligned role opens.

Thanks again for your time. Wishing you the best in your search.

Best,
Ben Carter
Technical Recruiter | Redwood Inference
